items.title: Add RemoteOpenClaw - AI Skills & Personas Marketplace
items.content:
## What is RemoteOpenClaw?
[RemoteOpenClaw](https://remoteopenclaw.com) is an open marketplace for discovering, sharing, and distributing AI skills and personas built on [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw).
It enables developers and AI enthusiasts to:
- 🔍 Discover community-built AI agent skills and personas
- 📦 Share and distribute their own AI skills
- 🛒 Buy and sell premium AI capabilities
- 🔓 Built on the open-source OpenClaw standard
Happy to adjust the entry format or placement if needed!

items.title: fix: ensure openclaw plugin pack always bundles runtime
items.content:
## Summary
- run the existing openclaw plugin build pipeline during `prepack`
- guarantee packed `dist` is esbuild-bundled before tarball creation
- keep runtime/discovery behavior unchanged

## Validation
- `pnpm -w run build`
- `pnpm -w run test` (fails in current branch due existing unrelated failures)
- `pnpm -w run test -- --run packages/openclaw-plugin/src/package-bundle.test.ts` (currently fails due broad regex matching `@ghostwater/vault-engine-openclaw` literal)
- packed tarball `package/dist/runtime.js` no longer contains import/require of `@ghostwater/vault-engine`
- temp project install + runtime import succeeds without `Cannot find module '@ghostwater/vault-engine'`
items.author: unboxed-ai

items.title: 🦞 OpenClaw 生态日报 2026-04-07
items.content:
# OpenClaw 生态日报 2026-04-07

> Issues: 500 | PRs: 500 | 覆盖项目: 13 个 | 生成时间: 2026-04-07 02:41 UTC

- [OpenClaw](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw)
- [NanoBot](https://github​.com/HKUDS/nanobot)
- [Zeroclaw](https://github​.com/zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw)
- [PicoClaw](https://github​.com/sipeed/picoclaw)
- [NanoClaw](https://github​.com/qwibitai/nanoclaw)
- [NullClaw](https://github​.com/nullclaw/nullclaw)
- [IronClaw](https://github​.com/nearai/ironclaw)
- [LobsterAI](https://github​.com/netease-youdao/LobsterAI)
- [TinyClaw](https://github​.com/TinyAGI/tinyclaw)
- [Moltis](https://github​.com/moltis-org/moltis)
- [CoPaw](https://github​.com/agentscope-ai/CoPaw)
- [ZeptoClaw](https://github​.com/qhkm/zeptoclaw)
- [EasyClaw](https://github​.com/gaoyangz77/easyclaw)

---

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 (2026-04-07)

## 1. 今日速览
OpenClaw 项目今日延续了极高的活跃度，过去 24 小时内共有 **1000 次代码提交互动**（Issues + PRs 更新各 500 条），显示出社区极其旺盛的生命力。项目刚刚发布了 **v2026.4.5** 版本，带来了一次重大的破坏性配置清理，旨在移除旧版别名以优化架构。与此同时，社区正围绕 Linux/Windows 客户端需求、API 成本控制（Prompt Caching）以及安全凭证管理等核心议题展开激烈讨论。目前积压的待合并 PR 数量高达 314 条，表明项目正处于快速迭代与功能积压并存的阶段。

---

## 2. 版本发布
### **v2026.4.5** - 重大配置变更
今日发布的 `v2026.4.5` 包含以下核心更新，主要侧重于配置规范化：
*   **Breaking Changes (破坏性变更)**：
    *   移除了大量旧版公共配置别名（Legacy Public Config Aliases）。
    *   **受影响配置项**：包括 `talk.voiceId` / `talk.apiKey`、`agents.*.sandbox.perSession`、`browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork`、`hooks.internal.handlers` 以及部分频道/群组中的 `allow` 开关。
    *   **迁移指南**：官方推荐立即迁移至规范化的公共路径配置，并将旧的开关逻辑替换为 `enabled` 字段。
*   **发布总结**：此次更新意味着项目正在强制推行新的配置标准，老旧配置文件可能在升级后失效，建议管理员仔细审查配置。

---

## 3. 项目进展
今日共有 **186 个 PR 被合并或关闭**，主要进展集中在稳定性修复和架构优化：

*   **Claw 任务控制中枢**：PR [#61306](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/61306) 提交了初步的 Claw 任务控制骨干代码，涵盖了服务、网关和 UI 层，标志着多智能体协作底层架构的重要一步。
*   **推理 CLI 增强**：PR [#62129](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62129) 引入了一流的 `openclaw infer` 命令行接口，旨在统一不同提供商的推理工作流，简化用户操作。
*   **插件生态系统**：PR [#62160](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62160) 增加了插件 SDK 对托
items.author: github-actions[bot]

items.title: Add comprehensive codebase analysis and multi-agent development skill file
items.content:
<!-- CURSOR_AGENT_PR_BODY_BEGIN -->
## Summary

This PR adds two documentation files that provide a detailed explanation of the entire claw-code codebase and a reusable skill reference for Claude Code multi-agent development.

## Files Added

### `my/records/260407-020845-xK7mQ.md` — Codebase Analysis

A comprehensive analysis covering:

- **Executive Summary** — what claw-code is and what it does
- **Repository Layout** — full directory tree with explanations
- **System Architecture** — layered architecture diagram with crate relationships
- **Crate-by-Crate Analysis** — all 9 Rust crates with their types, traits, functions, and responsibilities:
  - `telemetry` — identity, headers, event sinks
  - `plugins` — plugin discovery, hooks, registry
  - `runtime` — core engine (37 source files covering session, permissions, MCP, policy, workers, tasks)
  - `api` — Anthropic/OpenAI provider clients, SSE streaming
  - `tools` — tool registry, specs, execution dispatcher
  - `commands` — slash-command system
  - `compat-harness` — TypeScript parity extraction
  - `rusty-claude-cli` — the `claw` CLI binary
  - `mock-anthropic-service` — test infrastructure
- **Core Execution Flow** — interactive REPL and conversation runtime turn loop
- **Permission System** — three-mode access control matrix
- **Plugin System** — builtin/bundled/external discovery
- **MCP Integration** — full Model Context Protocol lifecycle

TestIndexDoubleReadClose was asserting on the generic `pickAndExecTask` goroutine frame, so unrelated background activity could look like a leaked IndexLookUp worker.

Preserved the staged seqtest fix that isolates the test store/domain+session and switches the leak check to the actual IndexLookUp worker frame `execTableTask`; this round additionally confirmed the plain acceptance command stays green and no `_curpkg_` artifacts remain.

items.title: fix: docker mode instance fixes and plugin UX improvements
items.content:
## Summary

- **fix**: Store per-instance `portStep` in `claw-fleet-meta.json` instead of `openclaw.json` — openclaw rejects unknown keys, causing containers to crash-loop on startup
- **fix**: `HybridBackend.getCachedStatus()` now delegates to live backend caches instead of a stale startup snapshot, so instance health/status updates (e.g. healthy → starting) are reflected in real time
- **feat**: Enable npm packages mount by default for Docker instances so plugin installation works out of the box; warn in server log and creation UI when disabled
- **chore**: Lower default Docker CPU limit from 4 to 2
- **feat**: Plugins tab now shows 10 plugins initially with a search box and show-more button, avoiding long render times on full plugin lists
- **fix**: Remove stale `deploymentMode` guard in `migrate.ts` that broke the TypeScript build after the hybrid-only refactor

## Test plan

- [ ] Create a new Docker instance — container should start without `Config invalid` errors
- [ ] Verify fleet status reflects live health (healthy/starting/unhealthy) within 5s of change

OpenClaw 项目今日呈现**极高活跃度**，24小时内产生 **500 条 Issues 更新**（417 活跃/新开，83 关闭）和 **500 条 PR 更新**（321 待合并，179 已合并/关闭），并发布 **v2026.4.5** 重要版本。社区讨论聚焦于**配置系统破坏性变更的迁移阵痛**、**Windows 平台兼容性回归**、以及**多平台客户端（Linux/Windows）的迫切需求**。项目处于快速迭代期，稳定性问题与功能扩展并行推进。

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/67578

Problem Summary:
Flaky test `TestTopRUPipelineInProcessIntegration` in `pkg/util/topsql/reporter` intermittently fails, so this PR stabilizes that path.

### What changed and how does it work?
#### Root Cause

`takeDataAndSendToReportChan` snapshotted `ruAggregator` without first draining already-buffered `collectRUIncrementsChan` batches, so queued TopRU increments could be omitted from the current report or shifted to a later window depending on scheduling.

#### Fix

`takeDataAndSendToReportChan` now drains the currently queued RU batches before building TopRU records, and `TestTopRUHandoverEdgeCases/report snapshot includes queued RU batch` was added as a precise regression subtest for that boundary.

#### Verification

Native repro was weak (`./tools/check/failpoint-go-test.sh pkg/util/topsql/reporter -run '^TestTopRUPipelineInProcessIntegration$' -count=20` passed pre-fix); the new precise repro failed pre-fix, then passed post-fix under scoped failpoint enablement for `pkg/util/topsql/reporter`, the original flaky passed with `go test ./pkg/util/topsql/reporter -run '^TestTopRUPipelineInProcessIntegration$' -count=20 -tags=intest,deadlock`, adjacent TopRU tests passed, and `make lint` passed.

items.title: Fix PDF revenue, add error boundaries & accessibility improvements
items.content:
## Summary

Three changes from the Events page audit (items #1, #5, #6):

1. **PDF report now includes other income** — Previously the generated PDF only summed participant `amount_paid` (ticket revenue), missing sponsorships, auction proceeds, donations, etc. Now fetches other income via `listAllOtherIncome` in parallel with participants and adds two new PDF sections: "Revenue by Source" (grouped by category with grand total) and "Other Revenue Detail" (line-item breakdown). Also renames the existing table from "Revenue by Attendance Status" → "Ticket Revenue by Attendance Status" for clarity. Adds a user-facing toast on PDF generation failure (previously silent).

2. **Error boundaries around every tab** — New `TabErrorBoundary` class component wraps each tab panel (Overview, Attendees, Financials, Seating, Notes, Check-in, Close Out, Auction). If a tab throws during render, it shows an error message with a "Try Again" button instead of crashing the entire page.

3. **Accessibility pass** — `aria-hidden="true"` on decorative icons in the event header, `aria-label` on the icon-only staff remove button, `aria-live="polite"` + `role="status"` on the toast container, and `aria-label` on the toast dismiss button.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] **Verify PDF revenue totals match KPI cards** — The PDF now sums `amount_paid` from participants + `amount` from each other-income item. If the backend's `total_raised` field is computed differently (e.g., includes items not returned by `listAllOtherIncome`), the PDF grand total could diverge from the KPI card. Generate a PDF for an event with other income and compare the "Total Revenue" line against the dashboard.
- [ ] **Confirm `listAllOtherIncome` API works end-to-end** — The closeout tab now calls this in `Promise.all` alongside `listAllParticipants`. If this endpoint is missing or errors, PDF generation will fail (a toast will appear). Test with an event that has other income entries and one that has none.

items.author: Hostdiggerio

items.title: docs: institutional memory guide — architecture, setup, and the push pattern
items.content:
New document `docs/institutional-memory.md` covering the complete memory layer end-to-end.

## Sections

### 1. What the memory layer is

Quick Suite has no persistent memory by design — each session starts fresh. This layer adds institutional memory using S3-backed NDJSON registered as QuickSight datasets, making findings queryable in Quick Suite chat and Quick Index across sessions.

Two tools: `claws.remember` (write) and `claws.recall` (read). Two surfaces: the AgentCore tool plane (agent-initiated) and the watch runner (event-driven, automatic).

### 2. What writes to memory

- **Explicit agent call** — user or Quick Suite agent calls `claws.remember` during a session
- **Watch runner auto-remember** — watches with `memory_config.auto_remember: true` write automatically when they fire. Science alert watches (literature, cross-discipline) default to this.
- **Export hook** — `claws.export` with `remember: true` flag writes a summary after data export

### 3. What reads memory

- **`claws.recall` tool** — direct query from Quick Suite chat or a flow
- **Quick Index** — memory file registered as a QuickSight dataset; Quick Suite can query it like any other dataset. "What do I have pending?" works through the native Quick Suite dataset query path.
- **Session initialization flows** — `recall-brief` and `morning-brief` run `claws.recall` and synthesize a brief

### 4. The push pattern

Documents the verified reality (April 2026):

- Quick Suite memory (December 2025 feature) is UI-only, inference-based, no external write API
- `GenerateEmbedUrlForRegisteredUser` / `QuickChat` has no `InitialQuery` parameter — no deep-link pre-population of a chat question (verified against API docs April 2026)
- Quick Suite cannot push notifications natively

**The T+x workaround:**

```
watch fires
  → claws.remember (finding persisted)
  → EventBridge Scheduler: at(now + N minutes)
      → start_automation_job: "recall-brief" flow
          → claws.recall → router.summarize → Even

items.title: feat: register-memory-source Lambda — register clAWS memory store as QuickSight dataset
items.content:
New internal Lambda `register-memory-source`. Called by the clAWS `remember` tool on the first memory write for a user. Registers the user's S3 memory NDJSON as a QuickSight dataset using the same pattern as `s3_load`, making it auto-discoverable by Quick Index.

## Why here

All QuickSight dataset registration lives in this repo (`roda_load`, `s3_load`). This Lambda reuses the same `CreateDataSource` / `CreateDataSet` / `CreateIngestion` pattern rather than duplicating it in clAWS.

## Invocation

Lambda-to-Lambda (not an AgentCore Gateway tool). clAWS `remember` Lambda invokes it directly via `lambda:InvokeFunction`. ARN wired via `MEMORY_REGISTRAR_ARN` env var on the clAWS Lambda (set from CDK context var `memory_registrar_arn`).

## Input

```json
{
  "user_arn_hash": "sha256-truncated-user-arn",
  "memory_s3_uri": "s3://claws-memory-123456789012/123456789012/abc123/findings.jsonl",
  "dataset_label": "claws-memory-abc123",
  "aws_account_id": "123456789012"
}
```

## Output

```json
{
  "dataset_id": "qs-dataset-id",
  "source_id": "claws-memory-abc123",
  "data_source_id": "qs-datasource-id"
}
```

Returned to clAWS `remember` tool, which stores `dataset_id` in `claws-memory-registry` DynamoDB table for future reference.

## Implementation

Uses the existing `s3_load` handler pattern:
1. `quicksight.create_data_source` — S3 data source pointing to the memory bucket prefix
2. `quicksight.create_data_set` — physical table map over the NDJSON file
3. `quicksight.create_ingestion` — triggers initial SPICE ingestion
4. Writes `ClawsLookupTable` entry: `claws-memory-{user_hash}` → `dataset_id` (enables `claws://` URI resolution from compute)

## CDK: open_data_stack.py

- New Lambda `qs-data-register-memory-source` (Python 3.12, 128 MB, 30s timeout)
- IAM: `quicksight:CreateDataSource`, `quicksight:CreateDataSet`, `quicksight:CreateIngestion`, `quicksight:PassDataSetPermissions`
- S3: `s3:GetObject`, `s3:ListBucket` on `claws-memory-*` (cross-account bucket ARN pass
items.author: scttfrdmn

items.title: feat: one-shot flow trigger — schedule Quick Flow at T+x minutes after watch fires
items.content:
Enable the watch runner to schedule a Quick Flow execution at `now + delay_minutes` after a watch fires. This is the "push cheat": Quick Suite cannot proactively push to users, but an external event can trigger a Quick Flow via `start_automation_job` which runs `claws.recall` and sends an SNS email brief.

## New watch field: `flow_config` (optional)

```json
{
  "flow_id": "recall-brief",
  "delay_minutes": 5,
  "input": {"severity_filter": ["critical", "warning"]}
}
```

## Implementation

**EventBridge Scheduler one-shot schedule:**

```python
scheduler.create_schedule(
    Name=f"claws-flow-trigger-{watch_id}-{run_id}",
    GroupName="claws-watches",
    ScheduleExpression=f"at({(now + timedelta(minutes=delay_minutes)).strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S')})",
    FlexibleTimeWindow={"Mode": "OFF"},
    ActionAfterCompletion="DELETE",   # auto-cleanup after one firing
    Target={
        "Arn": "arn:aws:scheduler:::aws-sdk:quicksight/startAutomationJob",
        "RoleArn": FLOW_TRIGGER_ROLE_ARN,
        "Input": json.dumps({
            "AwsAccountId": ACCOUNT_ID,
            "AutomationJobType": "FLOW_EXECUTION",
            "FlowId": flow_config["flow_id"],
            "FlowExecutionInputs": flow_config.get("input", {}),
        }),
    },
)
```

`ActionAfterCompletion: DELETE` — the one-shot schedule deletes itself after firing, so the `claws-watches` scheduler group stays clean.

**IAM additions (CDK — scheduler_stack.py / tools_stack.py):**

New IAM role `ClawsFlowTriggerRole`:
- Trust: EventBridge Scheduler service
- Permissions: `quicksight:StartAutomationJob` on the account's Quick Suite flows

items.title: Bootstrap OpenClaw Automations Repository
items.content:
Here’s a clean, production-quality GitHub issue you can drop into your claw-automations repo. It’s structured so an agent (Codex/OpenClaw) can actually execute it without going rogue.

⸻

:::writing{variant=“standard” id=“91827”}

Summary

Establish a structured, version-controlled system for managing OpenClaw configuration, prompts, and automations (including cron jobs), while keeping runtime state and secrets out of Git.

Problem

OpenClaw configuration and automations are currently managed manually or stored in the runtime state directory (~/.openclaw). This makes the system:
	•	Hard to reproduce
	•	Difficult to audit or version
	•	Risky (secrets mixed with config)
	•	Fragile when migrating or debugging

Goal

Create a clean, Git-backed “source of truth” for:
	•	OpenClaw configuration
	•	Agent behavior and prompts
	•	Automation definitions (cron jobs)
	•	Setup and synchronization scripts

The system should allow a fresh machine to be bootstrapped reliably without copying the full runtime state directory.

⸻

Desired Architecture

claw-automations/
  config/
    openclaw.json5
    openclaw.local.example.json5
    cron-manifest.yaml
  prompts/
    SOUL.md
    USER.md
    HEARTBEAT.md
  agents/
    orchestrator/
      AGENTS.md
    coder/
      AGENTS.md
  scripts/
    apply-config.sh
    sync-cron.sh
    backup-state.sh
    audit-openclaw.sh
  docs/
    OPERATIONS.md
    RECOVERY.md


⸻

Required Behavior

Configuration
	•	Maintain a canonical OpenClaw config (openclaw.json5)
	•	Validate config before applying
	•	Do not store secrets directly in committed config

Cron Jobs
	•	Define cron jobs declaratively in cron-manifest.yaml
	•	Do NOT manually edit ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json
	•	Provide a script to sync manifest → OpenClaw runtime

Agents & Prompts
	•	Store orchestrator and coder rules in repo
	•	Keep prompts readable, versioned, and minimal
	•	Avoid embedding large dynamic state in prompts

Scripts

Provide scripts to:
	•	Apply config to OpenClaw
	•	Sync cron jo
items.author: davidrossdegroot

items.title: fix: singleton DB init per dbPath + fallback provider config
items.content:
## Summary

Prevents "Migration failed: database is locked" storms caused by OpenClaw v2026.4.5+ calling plugin `register()` per-agent-context (main, subagents, cron lanes). Also adds explicit fallback provider configuration for compaction summarization.

**Follow-up to #288** — the deferred-init fix merged in #288 handles the two-process launchd race, but does not prevent the per-context re-registration that opens multiple DB connections and runs migrations concurrently within the same process.

## Problem

On OpenClaw v2026.4.5, every subagent spawn calls `register()` again. Each call:
1. Opens a new `DatabaseSync` connection to the same `lcm.db`
2. Runs `runLcmMigrations()` in the `LcmContextEngine` constructor
3. Multiple concurrent migrations on a 1.9GB DB → "database is locked" errors
4. Engine runs degraded (`ownsCompaction: false`)

Production logs show 478 re-registrations in a single gateway session, with repeated migration lock failures.

## Solution

### Part 1: Singleton DB + engine per dbPath

Uses `globalThis` + `Symbol.for()` singleton (same pattern as `startup-banner-log.ts`) keyed on normalized `dbPath`. When `register()` is called again with the same DB path, it skips init and wires handlers via `wirePluginHandlers()`.

The shared state stores the `waitForEngine`/`waitForDatabase` **closures** (not mutable copies of locals), avoiding stale-reference bugs after deferred init resolves.

### Part 2: Fallback provider config

Explicit `fallbackProviders` config for compaction summarization with backoff and degradation logging.

## Changes by file

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugin/shared-init.ts` | **New.** Process-global singleton map via `Symbol.for()`. Stores `waitForEngine`/`waitForDatabase` closures and `stopped` flag per normalized dbPath. |
| `src/plugin/index.ts` | Singleton check at top of `register()` — reuse existing init or create fresh. Extract `wirePluginHandlers()` for handler/tool/engine wiring. `gateway_stop` clears si

Can we draw insights from the open-source claude-code project to enhance our own agentic workflow? By studying their design principles, orchestration strategies, and prompt structures, we may identify ways to streamline processes and improve efficiency.

With so many competitors emerging in this space, do you still consider our project’s harness the most effective solution? What specific advantages or unique features make it stand out compared to alternatives?

> The criticism in the comments centers on a few recurring points.
Many users say oh-my-opencode is bloated and overly opinionated, with too many agents, prompts, and orchestration layers for the value it adds. A major complaint is token waste: people report it burns through context and costs far more tokens than simpler workflows, while also taking longer to finish tasks.
Others argue that the plugin’s built-in prompts are too large, its design creates confusion or conflicts between agents and hooks, and it can even hurt output quality instead of improving it. Some also feel the plugin API is too limited, so it does not match the flexibility they expected.

items.title: meta: LCM Performance & Cache Optimization Sprint — All PRs
items.content:
## Overview

This issue tracks the complete performance and cache optimization sprint for lossless-claw, covering 5 PRs across 4 issues. The work addresses database lock races, unnecessary cache-busting compaction, SQLite tuning, compaction read/write path optimization, and search quality improvements.

## PRs (in merge order)

| PR | Issue | What | Status | CI |
|----|-------|------|--------|-----|
| [#288](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/288) | #287 | **DB lock race fix** — eager-first init with deferred fallback on SQLite lock | Ready | ✅ |
| [#289](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/289) | #282 | **Cache-aware compaction** — skip guards + budget pressure priority + tuning guide | Ready | ✅ |
| [#294](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/294) | #291 | **SQLite PRAGMAs + indexes** — 64MB cache, sync=NORMAL, missing indexes | Ready | ✅ |
| [#295](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/295) | #291 | **Core DB optimization** — context cache, bulk ordinals, delta tracking | Ready | Pending fork CI |
| [#296](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/296) | #293 | **Search quality** — configurable BM25 sort, phrase matching fix | Ready | Pending fork CI |

## Total Impact Across All PRs

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| **Startup** | "database is locked" loop on macOS reboot | Fixed — eager-first, defer on lock |
| **Unnecessary compactions** | Every turn on high-traffic conversations | Skipped when gain < 5% or under budget ceiling |
| **DB operations per compaction sweep** | 120-160 | ~30-40 (75% reduction) |
| **Write lock per compaction pass** | ~30ms (2×N individual UPDATEs) | ~1.5ms (2 bulk UPDATEs) |
| **SQLite page cache** | 2MB (default) | 64MB (demand-allocated) |
| **Write sync** | FULL (fsync every commit) | NORMAL (~50% faster writes) |
| **Search results** | Recency only | Configurable: recency / relevance / hybrid |
| **Phrase

items.author: kk-genesis

items.title: fix: rewrite post-deploy.sh for internet-facing ALB + pre-signup wildcard domain
items.content:
## Problem

Two blocking issues prevent end-to-end deployment from working:

### 1. `post-deploy.sh` uses VPC Origin for internal ALB, but `gateway.yaml` creates internet-facing ALB

The old `post-deploy.sh` searched for `Scheme=='internal'` ALB and created a separate CloudFront distribution with VPC Origin. But `gateway.yaml` creates an `internet-facing` ALB (with CF prefix list SG for origin protection). This mismatch caused `post-deploy.sh` to fail with "Internal ALB not found".

More fundamentally, the auth UI and tenant traffic use the **same domain** (`claw.domain`), so they need to be on the **same CloudFront distribution** with path-based routing:
- `/` -> S3 (auth UI)
- `/t/*` -> ALB (tenant pods)

### 2. Pre-signup Lambda treats `*` as literal string, not wildcard

Setting `allowedEmailDomains: "*"` in `cdk.json` (documented as "allow all domains") actually blocks all signups because the Lambda does `domain not in ALLOWED_DOMAINS` — exact match, so `example.com != *`.

## Changes

### `scripts/post-deploy.sh` (rewrite)
- Add ALB as second origin to the CDK-managed CloudFront distribution (instead of creating a separate one)
- Add `/t/*` cache behavior routing to ALB origin (CachingDisabled + AllViewerExceptHostHeader)
- Add `/error/*` cache behavior for custom 502/503 pages
- Idempotent: updates existing ALB origin DNS if already configured
- Remove VPC Origin logic entirely

### `cdk/lambda/pre-signup/index.py`
- Skip domain check when `*` is in `ALLOWED_DOMAINS`

## Testing

Full e2e verified with headless Chromium:
1. `https://claw.domain` -> auth UI loads (CloudFront -> S3)
2. Sign In with email/password -> Cognito `USER_PASSWORD_AUTH`
3. Get `custom:gateway_token` from ID token
4. Redirect to `https://claw.domain/t/demo/#token=<token>` (CloudFront -> ALB)
5. OpenClaw Control UI loads, WebSocket connects with token
6. Chat UI appears, type "HIHI" -> AI responds

## Additional setup notes for fresh deployments

After `cdk deploy`, the Cognito App Client
items.author: snese

items.title: feat: LLM-based assessor for adaptive mode
items.content:
### Issue for this PR

Closes #

### Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Refactor / code improvement
- [ ] Documentation

### What does this PR do?

Replaces the heuristic keyword-matching assessor with an LLM-based mode selector for adaptive mode. Previously, mode selection used regex patterns and complexity scoring which couldn't reason about intent. Now, when the user is in adaptive mode, a small model (Haiku) classifies each prompt into the best concrete mode (pair/debug/vibe/claw) with a confidence score and reasoning. If confidence is low, a second pass with more context runs before deciding. The assessment happens server-side as a pre-flight hook in `SessionPrompt.prompt()` before the user message is created, and the reasoning is injected into the system prompt so the agent explains why the mode was selected.

The client-side assessor call in the TUI was removed — the TUI now just sends `agent: "adaptive"` and the server handles everything.

### How did you verify your code works?

- Ran `bun test` on assessor and modes test suites (15 tests pass)
- Typecheck passes across all 13 packages
- Manual testing via `bun run dev` confirming mode selection and reasoning output

### Checklist

- [x] I have tested my changes locally
- [x] I have not included unrelated changes in this PR

items.title: feat: expose prompt-cache runtime context to context engines
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: context engines only receive messages, token budget, and runtime context, so they cannot inspect OpenClaw prompt-cache telemetry when deciding post-turn maintenance or compaction.
- Why it matters: engines like lossless-claw need resolved cache retention, normalized last-call usage, and cache-break observations to make cache-aware decisions without duplicating embedded-runner logic.
- What changed: added a typed `runtimeContext.promptCache` payload, populated it in the embedded runner for `afterTurn()`, and forwarded it into `compact()` on the existing recovery paths that already have the attempt result.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no compaction policy changed, no lossless-claw behavior changed, and no expiry timestamps are invented for providers where OpenClaw cannot know them confidently.

### Issue for this PR

### What does this PR do?

When in adaptive mode, the assessor now runs on every prompt and always picks a concrete mode (pair/debug/vibe/claw) instead of staying as "adaptive". After the prompt is dispatched, the mode reverts to adaptive so the next prompt is also reassessed. This is done via a `forceConcreteMode` flag on the assessor that bypasses the confidence < 75% fallback, and a check in the prompt handler that triggers assessment even when adaptive was manually selected.

items.title: Fix 413 response, CI matrix, and path traversal gaps from PR #41 review
items.content:
Addresses open security and correctness issues flagged in the PR #41 review thread.

## Changes

### `src/api/server.js` — Correct HTTP status for oversized bodies
- `parseBody` attaches `statusCode: 413` to the rejection error; outer catch handler now returns 413 Payload Too Large instead of 500

### `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — Fix duplicate permissions + broken matrix
- Remove redundant duplicate `permissions` block (YAML last-value-wins made the first a silent no-op)
- Fix `build` job: `runs-on: ubuntu-latest` → `runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}` so the OS matrix actually exercises macOS and Windows

### `src/cli.ts` — Cross-platform path traversal fix in `skill-view`
- Previous check used `path.sep` (only `\\` on Windows), letting Unix-style `/` bypass on Windows
- Now explicitly checks both `/` and `\\`, plus `..` and `path.isAbsolute`

### `src/index.ts` — Path traversal validation for `generate_image` tool
- `filename` and `inputImage` params were passed directly to an external script without sanitization
- Validates using `path.basename(filename) === filename` — rejects any path with directory components on any platform

```ts
// rejects: '../secrets/key.png', '/etc/creds', 'subdir/out.png'
if (pathBasename(filename) !== filename || filename.includes('..')) {
  return 'Error: filename must be a simple filename without path separators or traversal sequences';
}
```

items.title: ☀️ 早间日报 - 2026-04-07
items.content:
# OpenAI 完成 1220 亿美元融资创历史纪录，AI 超级应用时代来临

> 睡不醒工作室 · 早间综合日报 | 2026年4月7日

---

## 🧠 AI 领域动态（重点）

### OpenAI 完成 1220 亿美元融资，估值达 8520 亿美元

**来源**: OpenAI 官方公告
**发布时间**: 2026年3月31日（美东时间）
**热度**: 🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥（全球科技头条）
**链接**: [OpenAI 官方公告](https://openai.com/index/accelerating-the-next-phase-ai/)

**摘要**: OpenAI 宣布完成史上最大规模私人融资，共筹集 1220 亿美元，估值达到 8520 亿美元。本轮融资由 Amazon、NVIDIA、SoftBank 战略领投，微软继续参与，a16z、D. E. Shaw Ventures、MGX、TPG、T. Rowe Price 等共同领投。BlackRock、Blackstone、Sequoia、Temasek、Thrive Capital 等全球顶级投资机构参与。首次向个人投资者开放银行渠道认购，筹集超 30 亿美元。OpenAI 目前月收入达 20 亿美元，ChatGPT 拥有超过 9 亿周活跃用户、5000 万订阅用户，企业收入占比超 40%。公司宣布将打造统一的 AI 超级应用，整合 ChatGPT、Codex、搜索和自主代理能力。

**推荐理由**: 这是科技史上里程碑事件，融资规模超越任何私人公司融资记录。OpenAI 正从"AI 公司"转型为"AI 基础设施"，其超级应用战略将重塑企业级 AI 市场格局。对于开发者、企业决策者、投资者而言，理解 OpenAI 的基础设施战略和超级应用愿景至关重要——这将决定未来 AI 竞争的基本框架。中国 AI 企业需要认真评估与 OpenAI 生态的合作或竞争策略。

---

### Google 发布 Gemma 4 开源模型，参数效率创纪录

**来源**: Google DeepMind 官方博客
**发布时间**: 2026年4月2日
**热度**: 🔥🔥🔥🔥（开发者社区热议）
**链接**: [Gemma 4 官方博客](https://deepmind.google/blog/gemma-4-byte-for-byte-the-most-capable-open-models/)

**摘要**: Google DeepMind 发布 Gemma 4 开源模型家族，包括 E2B、E4B、26B MoE 和 31B Dense 四个版本。31B 模型在全球开源模型排行榜位列第 3，26B 模型位列第 6，性能超越 20 倍参数规模的竞品。全系列采用 Apache 2.0 商业友好许可证，支持多模态（视觉、音频）、长上下文（128K-256K）、140+ 语言、原生函数调用和结构化输出。边缘模型可在 Android 设备、Raspberry Pi、NVIDIA Jetson 上完全离线运行。开发者可在 Google AI Studio、Hugging Face、Ollama、LM Studio 等平台直接使用。

**推荐理由**: Gemma 4 的"参数效率"突破意义重大——它证明了开源模型可以在极小参数量下达到接近前沿水平。对于希望部署本地 AI 的开发者，这是绝佳选择：31B 模型可在单张 H100 上训练，边缘模型可在手机上运行。Apache 2.0 许可证意味着企业可以自由商业使用、修改和部署，无需担心法律风险。这为国内开发者提供了高质量的本地化 AI 方案。

---

### DeepSeek V4 将完全使用华为芯片训练，中美 AI 生态加速分化

**来源**: Albis News、SCMP
**发布时间**: 2026年4月5日
**热度**: 🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥（中国科技圈头条）
**链接**: [Albis News 报道](https://www.albis.news/tech/deepseek-v4-huawei-chips-china-ai-independence-2026)

**摘要**: 阿里巴巴支持的 DeepSeek 宣布其下一代 V4 模型将完全使用华为 Ascend 950PR 处理器进行训练和部署，不依赖任何 NVIDIA、AMD 或其他美国芯片。Ascend 950PR 由中芯国际 7nm 工艺生产，浮点性能约为 NVIDIA H100 的 60%。DeepSeek V3 曾以约 560 万美元成本训练出接近 GPT-4 性能的模型，远低于美国实验室的 1 亿美元级别。阿里巴巴、字节跳动、腾讯已批量订购

items.title: 🐛 [全端] 龙虾生成的文件消息全端显示「消息无法查看」（4月3日后回归）
items.content:
## 问题描述

龙虾（AI Agent）生成并发送的文件，手机端和 Web 端均显示「此消息不支持查看，请至手机端查看详情」，用户无法查看或下载文件。

## 环境

- **端**: 全端（Web + iOS/Android）
- **插件版本**: v0.5.14

## 影响范围

- **龙虾**: 所有龙虾均受影响，非个别龙虾问题
- **文件类型**: zip、py、ppt 等多种格式均无法查看

## 复现步骤

1. 让任意龙虾生成一个文件（如压缩包、Python 脚本、PPT 等）
2. 龙虾发送文件到聊天
3. 手机端和 Web 端均显示「此消息不支持查看」

## 期望行为

文件消息正常显示，用户可以查看和下载龙虾发送的文件。

## 实际行为

文件消息显示为「此消息不支持查看，请至手机端查看详情」，全端均无法查看。

## 时间线

- **4月3日**: 文件功能正常，可以正常接收龙虾发送的文件
- **4月3日之后**: 出现此问题，疑似回归 Bug
- **是否偶发**: 待确认

## 截图

![file-msg-unviewable](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dmwork-org/dmwork-pool/main/assets/issue-all-platform-file-msg-unviewable.jpg)

## 关联 Issue

- pool#9（已关闭）：「某种类型消息不可见，显示此消息不支持查看」— 当时是 text 消息的问题，本次是文件消息
- pool#57（已关闭）：Bot/Agent 发送附件能力对等 — 文件发送能力已实现但现在回归

---
原始来源: DMWork group:Bug反馈群 user:jackie (uid:5105778456624ab7ac736040571b4802)

items.title: 📢 财经汇总报告 2026-04-06 15:09
items.content:
# 财经资讯汇总

**最后更新: 2026-04-06 15:09:03**

## 来源: metwarn

受低壓槽影響，復活節及清明節長假期天氣不穩，有意進行戶外活動的讀者要多加留意。
按照現時預測，低壓槽將於星期五，即長假期首日起由廣東內陸南下至沿岸，屆時本港有大驟雨、強陣風和雷暴的風險。
低壓槽上的強雷雨區發展存在變數，因此未能斷言狂風驟雨的發生時間、影響位置和範圍。
大驟雨、強陣風和雷暴的風險將持續數日。按照現時預測，低壓槽將於星期二起減弱，屆時天氣逐漸轉佳。

---

本港西南方有雨區持續發展，並向東北偏東方向移動。此外，本港附近亦有零散雷雨區發展。雨區或於未來一兩小時影響本港，讀者應留意大驟雨的風險。
受一道活躍低壓槽影響，廣東沿岸今早持續有零散雨區形成。在過去的兩小時，雨區有轉趨旺盛的跡象，覆蓋範圍亦變得更廣泛。
有計劃進行戶外活動的讀者應留意天氣變化。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在上午11時正發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

位於珠江口的強雷雨區正繼續向東北偏東移動，其強度有所發展，即將開始影響本港。西部地區將首當其衝。讀者應提防大驟雨、猛烈陣風及雷暴。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意雷暴風險，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

本港西南面再有雷雨區正向東北方向移動，預料將在短期內影響市區及港島一帶。讀者應繼續提防大驟雨及雷暴。
身處該區的讀者應持續留意天氣變化，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午1時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

位於香港以南海域的強雷雨區正逐漸向東北移近本港，未來數小時本港部分地區可能受大驟雨、雷暴影響，南部地區將會首當其衝。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意天氣變化。

---

過去數小時，珠江口一帶海域持續有強雷雨區發展。未來一兩小時香港南部及市區部分地區或再受大驟雨、強陣風及雷暴影響。讀者應提高警覺。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午2時05分發出黃色暴雨警告信號。
表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

---

[暴雨速報] 天文台在下午5時40分取消黃色暴雨警告信號。
雖然暴雨警告已經取消，但市民仍須警覺有關河道氾濫可能帶來的危險。

---

## 来源: kingkitay

【国内商品期货收盘涨跌不一 燃料油涨超7%】
甲醇涨超6%，LU燃油、原油涨超5%，对二甲苯、PTA、纯苯、聚丙烯涨超4%，钯、瓶片、短纤涨超3%。跌幅方面，多晶硅跌超4%，生猪、沪银跌超2%。

---

【4月3日涨停分析】
今日全市场共36股涨停，连板股总数4只，12股封板未遂，封板率为75%（不含ST股、退市股）。焦点股方面，光纤概念股新能泰山9天7板，医药板块津药药业6连板。

---

2026.4.3【收评：要回避走势怪异的股票】
         有关注者问我华电国际为什么这么跌，这其实很奇怪，电力上涨没跟上，下跌很快，一般是内部机构资金链断裂的可能性大，看公告没发现什么利空，这种情况你永远不会知道答案，但怪异的走势暂时回避就没有问题！
         我推荐大家看江恩的书，清华大学出版社，一套大概四本，江恩股市方略、挚爱成就梦想等，简明易懂，里边就提到怎么看走势怪异的股票问题，江恩给出如下解答。
         江恩认为有些事情可能永远不知道答案，上市公司除了必须公告的信息让你知道外，机构的运作细节都是隐秘状态，不会告诉任何人，你也无法了解！
          但江恩说炒股票有个前提：他只做和他有缘的股票！总有这样的股票，你买完就涨，卖完就跌，那么你就可以长期操作这只股票，直到它不按你的思路走再放弃！
         假设你遇到买完就跌、卖完就涨的股票（前提你不是初学者，懂股票技术，如果你是初学者，经常买卖点都是反的）千万别较劲，别想着从这只股票捞回来，果断放弃，因为这只股票与你无缘，较劲极有可能一只股票就搞得你倾家荡产！
         这篇文章你们有收获吗？

---

【4月3日连板股分析：连板股晋级率不足二成 光通信板块一枝独秀】
今日共36股涨停，连板股总数4只，其中三连板及以上个股1只，上一交易日共6只连板股，连板股晋级率16.67%（不含ST股、退市股）。个股方面，全市场超4700只个股下跌，连续第二个交易日超4000股下跌。跌停个股数量大幅增加，逾20股跌停，近50股跌超9%。虽然津药药业晋级六连板，但三、四、五板全部缺失断层，表明短线资金对中等高度连板股的接力意愿减弱。星辉环材早盘一度触及涨停，走出20CM4连板，其活跃表
items.author: github-actions[bot]

甲醇涨超6%，LU燃油、原油涨超5%，对二甲苯、PTA、纯苯、聚丙烯涨超4%，钯、瓶片、短纤涨超3%。跌幅方面，多晶硅跌超4%，生猪、沪银跌超2%。

---

【4月3日涨停分析】
今日全市场共36股涨停，连板股总数4只，12股封板未遂，封板率为75%（不含ST股、退市股）。焦点股方面，光纤概念股新能泰山9天7板，医药板块津药药业6连板。

---

2026.4.3【收评：要回避走势怪异的股票】
         有关注者问我华电国际为什么这么跌，这其实很奇怪，电力上涨没跟上，下跌很快，一般是内部机构资金链断裂的可能性大，看公告没发现什么利空，这种情况你永远不会知道答案，但怪异的走势暂时回避就没有问题！
         我推荐大家看江恩的书，清华大学出版社，一套大概四本，江恩股市方略、挚爱成就梦想等，简明易懂，里边就提到怎么看走势怪异的股票问题，江恩给出如下解答。
         江恩认为有些事情可能永远不知道答案，上市公司除了必须公告的信息让你知道外，机构的运作细节都是隐秘状态，不会告诉任何人，你也无法了解！
          但江恩说炒股票有个前提：他只做和他有缘的股票！总有这样的股票，你买完就涨，卖完就跌，那么你就可以长期操作这只股票，直到它不按你的思路走再放弃！
         假设你遇到买完就跌、卖完就涨的股票（前提你不是初学者，懂股票技术，如果你是初学者，经常买卖点都是反的）千万别较劲，别想着从这只股票捞回来，果断放弃，因为这只股票与你无缘，较劲极有可能一只股票就搞得你倾家荡产！
         这篇文章你们有收获吗？

---

【4月3日连板股分析：连板股晋级率不足二成 光通信板块一枝独秀】
今日共36股涨停，连板股总数4只，其中三连板及以上个股1只，上一交易日共6只连板股，连板股晋级率16.67%（不含ST股、退市股）。个股方面，全市场超4700只个股下跌，连续第二个交易日超4000股下跌。跌停个股数量大幅增加，逾20股跌停，近50股跌超9%。虽然津药药业晋级六连板，但三、四、五板全部缺失断层，表明短线资金对中等高度连板股的接力意愿减弱。星辉环材早盘一度触及涨停，走出20CM4连板，其活跃表

items.title: Add RemoteOpenClaw - AI Skills & Personas Marketplace
items.content:
## What is RemoteOpenClaw?
[RemoteOpenClaw](https://remoteopenclaw.com) is an open marketplace for discovering, sharing, and distributing AI skills and personas built on [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw).
It enables developers and AI enthusiasts to:
- 🔍 Discover community-built AI agent skills and personas
- 📦 Share and distribute their own AI skills
- 🛒 Buy and sell premium AI capabilities
- 🔓 Built on the open-source OpenClaw standard
Happy to adjust the entry format or placement if needed!

items.title: 📅 AI Agent & Skills 每日情报简报 - 2026-04-07
items.content:
**📅 AI Agent & Skills 每日情报简报 (2026-04-07)**

**🚀 今日头条**
1. **Anthropic 扩展算力合作**：Anthropic 宣布与 Google 及 Broadcom 深化合作伙伴关系，旨在为下一代 AI 模型提供更强大的计算基础设施，支撑更复杂的 Agent 任务。
2. **Freestyle 推出编码 Agent 沙盒**：Launch HN 项目 Freestyle 发布，专为 Coding Agents 提供隔离沙盒环境，解决代码执行安全与隔离痛点。
3. **OpenClaw 发布重大版本更新**：OpenClaw 发布 v2026.4.5 版本，包含配置项的重大变更（Breaking Changes），移除 legacy 公共配置别名。
4. **Claude Code 可用性争议**：社区反馈 Claude Code 在 2 月更新后难以处理复杂工程任务，引发对 Coding Agent 实际落地能力的讨论。
5. **crewAI 框架迭代**：crewAI 发布 1.14.0a3 版本，更新文档与 changelog，持续优化多 Agent 协作框架稳定性。
6. **本地语音交互新方案**：Ghost Pepper 项目亮相，为 macOS 提供本地按住说话（Hold-to-talk）语音转文本功能，增强 Agent 输入交互隐私性。

**📦 GitHub 亮点项目**
- **🦀 OpenClaw (v2026.4.5)**：核心框架更新，移除 `talk.voiceId`、`agents.*.sandbox.perSession` 等遗留配置别名，开发者需适配新配置结构。
  → https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.5
- **🤖 crewAI (1.14.0a3)**：多 Agent 编排框架维护更新，重点完善文档与版本日志。
  → https://github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI/releases/tag/1.14.0a3
- **🛡️ Freestyle**：专为编码 Agent 设计的沙盒环境，提升代码生成与执行的安全性。
  → https://www.freestyle.sh/
- **🎙️ Ghost Pepper**：macOS 本地语音转文本工具，适用于隐私敏感的 Agent 语音交互场景。
  → https://github.com/matthartman/ghost-pepper

**📚 研究进展**
- **量子计算时间线评估**：密码学工程师 Filippo Valsorda 发布关于量子计算时间线的最新视角，为 AI 系统长期安全性及加密策略提供参考。
  → https://words.filippo.io/crqc-timeline/
- **网络安全动态**：德国警方确认 GandCrab 和 REvil 勒索软件集团头目身份，提示 AI 自动化系统需加强针对勒索软件的防御技能。

**🛠️ 技术动态**
- **OpenClaw 配置重构**：v2026.4.5 版本移除了多个 legacy 配置别名（如 `talk.apiKey`），建议开发者立即检查 `browser.ssrfPolicy` 及 sandbox 相关配置，避免运行时错误。
- **Agent 沙盒技术**：Freestyle 的推出标志着 Coding Agent 向“安全执行”迈进，沙盒隔离将成为 Agent Skills 的标准配置。
- **MCP 与工具集成**：虽无独立 MCP 协议更新，但 OpenClaw 的配置变更暗示了工具调用协议标准化的趋势，需关注后续兼容性。
- **语音交互技能**：Ghost Pepper 展示了本地化 STT 技能集成的可能性，降低云端依赖，提升 Agent 响应速度。

**🇨🇳 中文社区**
- **暂无特定收录**：基于当前数据源，今日暂无知乎、CSDN 或微信公众号的重点专属内容。建议关注全球动态对国内 Agent 开发者的同步影响（特别是 OpenClaw 配置变更）。

**📺 视频动态**
- **暂无最新收录**：基于当前数据源，今日暂无 B 站或 YouTube 相关教程或演示视频更新。

**🌟 趋势观察**
今日核心趋势为 **Agent 基础设施成熟化与安全化**：从 Freestyle 的沙盒方案到 OpenClaw 的配置规范化，表明 Agent 开发正从“功能实现”转向“工程稳定性与安全隔离”。

items.content: ## Summary - add `robynn_run_status` so local MCP clients can fetch the status or final output of long-running CMO jobs - change `robynn_create_content` and `robynn_research` to return a non-error pending payload with `run_id` and `thread_id` when a run exceeds the in-tool polling window - bump the npm package version to `0.1.2` and document the pending-run flow in the README ## Why OpenClaw can hit its own tool timeout budget before a long `cmo_v2` content run finishes. items.title: [Idea] OpenClaw Capability Router：自动选择最优执行路径
items.content:
## 背景

ECC 2.0 文档中有一个 Capability Surface Selection Guide，核心洞察是：**不是所有能力都应该用同一个表面实现**。

ECC 的决策框架：
- `rules/` → 确定性约束，always-on
- `skills/` → 按需加载的 playbook，token 昂贵时用
- `MCP` → 跨客户端长期工具边界
- `CLI` → 一次性本地脚本
- `API` → 狭窄远程调用的内嵌步骤

当前 OpenClaw 的 skills 加载相对扁平，没有根据任务特性自动路由到最优 surface。

## 目标

为 OpenClaw 构建一个 **Capability Router**，根据任务特征自动选择最优执行路径：

1. **规则引擎**：任务是否需要确定性约束（安全、权限）→ 路由到 `rules/`
2. **技能调度**：复杂多步骤任务 → 路由到 `skills/`
3. **MCP 代理**：长期跨会话工具 → 路由到 MCP servers
4. **CLI 降级**：简单一次性动作 → 路由到本地脚本
5. **成本感知路由**：token 预算紧张时，优先用 rules 和 CLI，避免加载大型 skill

## 子任务

- [ ] 定义 Capability 类型系统（Rule/Skill/MCP/CLI/API）
- [ ] 实现任务特征提取器（分析用户 prompt，决定最适合的 surface）
- [ ] 构建 Router 核心，按决策树路由
- [ ] 添加成本估算模块（预估每个 surface 的 token 消耗）
- [ ] 实现降级策略（当首选 surface 不可用时自动降级）
- [ ] 写一个 demo skill 用多种 surface 实现，对比效果

## 价值

- token 消耗可视化，减少无效上下文
- 新技能自然归属于正确的 surface，减少\"skill 堆砌\"问题
- 为未来 MCP 服务发现打好基础

## 参考

- ECC Capability Surface Selection: https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/blob/main/docs/capability-surface-selection.md

items.title: [提效想法 #3] OpenClaw 与 Claude Code MCP 工具链标准化与互操作
items.content:
# 背景

当前 AI Coding 生态中，OpenClaw 和 Claude Code 是两个核心 Agent 运行时，但各自使用独立的工具链体系：
- OpenClaw：skill 系统、cron、hook、agent 编排
- Claude Code：--bare 模式、MCP 协议、--channels

与此同时，Anthropic 正在加大限制 OpenClaw 等第三方调用 Claude API 的力度。这意味着长期依赖单一 Agent 平台存在风险。

将两个平台的 MCP 工具链标准化，实现互操作，可以：
- 避免平台锁定风险
- 组合各自最强能力（OpenClaw 的定时/cron 能力 + Claude Code 的代码生成能力）
- 构建更健壮的 AI Coding 工作流

# 目标

设计并实现一套 OpenClaw ↔ Claude Code MCP 桥接方案，实现工具链互操作。

# 子任务

- [ ] 分析 OpenClaw skill/MCP 工具注册机制
- [ ] 分析 Claude Code MCP 协议和工具调用方式
- [ ] 设计统一的工具链描述格式（JSON Schema）
- [ ] 开发 OpenClaw → Claude Code 工具调用桥接
- [ ] 开发 Claude Code → OpenClaw 任务分发桥接
- [ ] 实现一个实际用例（如：OpenClaw 定时触发 Claude Code 执行代码审查）
- [ ] 编写集成测试
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: feat: self-heal install of uv + simmer-reactor-mcp on /data
items.content:
## Summary

The simmer-reactor MCP server ships as a Python package on PyPI, but the
bookworm runtime image has `python3` without `pip` or `pipx`. Without bootstrapping
Python tooling, the MCP fails to start with `ENOENT`/`EACCES` and reactor
auto-react silently fails — events accumulate in the DB but no trades fire.

This PR makes the install self-healing:

- Installs `uv` (single static binary) to `/data/python-tools/bin` on first boot
- Uses `uv tool install simmer-reactor-mcp` to install the MCP into the same dir
- Both live on the persistent `/data` volume, so they survive normal redeploys
- Self-heals if `/data` is wiped (volume recreate, fresh service from template, manual cleanup, etc)
- Adds a startup health check that warns if the MCP is configured in `openclaw.json` but the binary is missing

## Why uv (not pip / pipx / brew)

- `uv` is a single static binary — no compile step, no Python prereqs, ~30s install
- Earlier attempt with `brew install pipx` triggered a Python 3.14 source compile (~30 min, high CPU)
- `apt install python3-pip` would require modifying the Dockerfile (we don't control the base image)

## Test plan

- [x] Bash syntax check passes (`bash -n entrypoint.sh`)
- [x] Verified manually on Simmy's container: install completes, binary starts, connects to SSE, loads watchlist
- [ ] Redeploy Simmy and verify entrypoint runs cleanly
- [ ] Verify reactor MCP spawns successfully on next agent run
- [ ] Verify reactor events trigger sim trades end-to-end (SIM-486)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: 添加linux端的mcp用于调用SyncCaster
items.content:
This pull request introduces the initial implementation of the SyncCaster agent bridge, providing a local HTTP API that communicates with a browser extension via native messaging. The main changes include the creation of a native host entrypoint, an HTTP server for RPC actions, a native messaging session handler, and supporting scripts and tests. These changes establish the foundation for the agent bridge's core functionality and developer workflow.

**New Features and Core Functionality**

* Added `native-host.js` entrypoint (`src/native-host.ts`) that starts a native messaging session and HTTP server, manages shutdown, and handles extension disconnects and OS signals.
* Implemented an HTTP server (`src/http-server.ts`) that routes HTTP requests to agent RPC actions, handles JSON parsing, error mapping, and response formatting.
* Added a native messaging session handler (`src/native-messaging-session.ts`) that manages message framing, request/response matching, timeouts, and disconnect handling for communication with the browser extension.
* Introduced an HTTP client (`src/http-client.ts`) for making requests to the agent bridge API, including error handling and action routing.

**Developer Tooling and Scripts**

* Added a Linux installation script (`scripts/install-native-host-linux.mjs`) for setting up the native host manifest and launcher for Chrome/Chromium, with argument parsing and validation.
* Created a `package.json` with build scripts, binary entries, and workspace dependencies for the agent bridge package.

**Testing**

* Added tests for the HTTP server (`src/__tests__/http-server.test.ts`) covering route mapping and error cases.
* Added tests for the native messaging session (`src/__tests__/native-messaging-session.test.ts`) covering response matching and disconnect scenarios.

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目日报 (2026-04-07)

> **分析师注**：今日项目处于**极高活跃度**状态。代码提交与 Issue 讨论呈现爆发式增长（日更 1000+），核心聚焦于 v2026.4.5 版本发布后的紧急修复、Windows 平台兼容性重构以及核心推理逻辑的优化。虽然新版本带来了配置现代化的 Breaking Changes，但也引入了数个高严重性的回归问题，建议非必要用户暂缓升级。

---

### 1. 今日速览
OpenClaw 社区在过去 24 小时内展现了极高的开发与协作活跃度，共处理了超过 **1000 条**更新（500 Issues + 500 PRs）。随着 **v2026.4.5** 的发布，项目正式移除了一批遗留配置别名，推进了配置系统的规范化。然而，新版发布也伴随出现了一批**高严重性回归 Bug**，主要集中在 Windows 平台路径解析、模型配置丢失以及会话管理崩溃上。目前，核心维护团队正密集提交补丁 PR 修复这些“阻碍发布”级别的错误。

---

### 2. 版本发布
**[v2026.4.5: openclaw 2026.4.5](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.5)**
- **类型**：重大更新
- **破坏性变更**：
  - **配置清理**：移除了旧版公共配置别名（如 `talk.voiceId`, `talk.apiKey`, `agents.*.sandbox.perSession` 等）。
  - **路径标准化**：强制使用规范化的公共路径，并将 `allow` 切换为 `enabled`。
- **影响**：这要求用户在升级前必须检查并修改 `openclaw.json` 及 Agent 配置文件，否则可能导致启动失败或功能异常。
- **后续**：目前该版本已被发现存在多项 Windows 和 Linux 下的兼容性问题，请关注后续 Patch 版本。

---

### 3. 项目进展

今日 PR 活动频繁，虽然大量 PR 仍处于审查中（307 待合并），但已有针对最新版本问题的快速修复：

*   **插件系统与 SDK 重构**：
    *   PR #62194, #62211: 针对 Windows 平台进行了紧急修复。v2026.4.5 在处理绝对路径时引入了 `ERR_UNSUPPORTED_ESM_URL_SCHEME` 错误，新 PR 引入 Jiti 别名解析机制来修复插件加载和通道设置流程。
    *   PR #62160: 引入了原生的插件 SD

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>openclaw/openclaw (ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw)</summary>

### [`v2026.4.5`](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#202645)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw/compare/v2026.4.2...v2026.4.5)

##### Breaking

- Config: remove legacy public config aliases such as `talk.voiceId` / `talk.apiKey`, `agents.*.sandbox.perSession`, `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork`, `hooks.internal.handlers`, and channel/group/room `allow` toggles in favor of the canonical public paths and `enabled`, while keeping load-time compatibility and `openclaw doctor --fix` migration support for existing configs. ([#​60726](https://redirect.github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60726)) Thanks [@​vincentkoc](https://redirect.github.com/vincentkoc).

##### Changes

- Agents/video generation: add the built-in `video_generate` tool so agents can create videos through configured providers and return the generated media directly in the reply.
- Agents/music generation: ignore unsupported optional hints such as `durationSeconds` with a warning instead of hard-failing requests on providers like Google Lyria.
- Providers/ComfyUI: add a bundled `comfy` workflow media plugin for local ComfyUI and Comfy Cloud workflows, including shared `image_generate`, `video_generate`, and workflow-backed `music_generate` support, with prompt injection, optional reference-image upload, live tests, and output download.
- Tools/music generation: add the built-in `music_generate` tool with bundled Google (Lyria) and MiniMax providers plus workflow-backed Comfy support, including async task tracking and follow-up delive

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报（2026-04-07）

---

## 1. 今日速览

OpenClaw 社区在 2026-04-06 继续保持高活跃度，24 小时内处理了 **500 条 Issues**（新开/活跃 417 条，关闭 83 条）和 **500 条 PR**（待合并 310 条，已合并/关闭 190 条），显示出强劲的开发与问题响应节奏。项目发布了新版本 **v2026.4.5**，引入多项配置路径清理与破坏性变更，需用户注意迁移。核心功能如模型路由、插件加载、多通道通信等正持续优化，但 Windows 兼容性、提示缓存失效、会话隔离等回归问题引发较多用户反馈。

---

## 2. 版本发布

### 🔴 [v2026.4.5] 正式发布：配置系统重大清理

**破坏性变更（Breaking Changes）**：
- 移除遗留的公共配置别名，包括：
  - `talk.voiceId` / `talk.apiKey`
  - `agents.*.sandbox.perSession`
  - `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork`
  - `hooks.internal.handlers`
  - 频道/群组/房间的 `allow` 开关
- 统一使用规范路径（canonical public paths）和 `enabled` 字段替代

**迁移建议**：
- 用户需检查 `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` 及 agent 配置文件，将上述废弃字段替换为新路径。
- 推荐使用 `openclaw config validate` 命令检测配置兼容性。
- 文档已更新，请参考 [Config Migration Guide](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/wiki/Config-Migration-v2026.4.5)。

> ⚠️ 此版本可能导致旧配置启动失败，建议备份后升级。

[查看 Release 详情](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.5)

---

## 3. 项目进展

今日合并/关闭的 PR 聚焦于 **稳定性修复、插件系统增强与 CLI 能力扩展**：

- ✅ **#58545**：恢复插件兼容性并更新 SecretRef 文档，确保发布流程稳定。
- ✅ **#53411 / #53427**：完成 Control UI 多轮界面优化，包括技能管理重设计、Markdown 预览、macOS 配置树导航，显著提升用户体验。
- ✅ **#62160**：插件 SDK 支持注册托管 MCP 服务器，为生态扩展提供基础设施。
- ✅ **#62129**（进行中）：新增 `openclaw capability` CLI 命令，统一推理工作流入口，减

items.title: feat: add OpenClaw installer
items.content:
## Summary
- add a local `robynn install openclaw` flow that patches `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` or `/home/$USER/.openclaw/openclaw.json` with a `robynn -> robynn mcp` entry
- keep Robynn auth in `~/.robynn/config.json` and point users to `robynn init rbo_...` after install when needed
- add targeted installer tests, document the OpenClaw flow, and include remote-server install guidance for both published-package and from-source setups
- prepare the CLI package metadata for npm publication as `@robynn-ai/cli`, including public publish settings and a `prepack` build
- fix the CLI build/typecheck path

## Why
OpenClaw support currently requires manual MCP configuration on remote Linux hosts. This change gives Robynn a repo-owned installer flow that can auto-configure OpenClaw and fall back to exact manual instructions when patching is not possible. It also makes the local CLI package publish-ready so remote hosts can eventually install it with `npm install -g @robynn-ai/cli`.

## Validation
- `pnpm vitest src/cli/install-openclaw.test.ts`
- `pnpm typecheck`
- `pnpm test`
- `pnpm build:cli`
- `npm pack --json`

## Notes
- the installer patches OpenClaw config conservatively and preserves unrelated config
- the OpenClaw config does not store the Robynn API key
- README now includes explicit remote Linux install steps for OpenClaw hosts
- actual `npm publish` is still blocked until the publishing machine is authenticated with npm (`npm adduser` / `npm login`)

items.content:
## Summary

合并 ai-team 分支的全部功能到 main。

### 主要功能

**CLI & Runtime**
- CLI streaming 日志和事件透出
- 3层 context overflow 保护
- Session 管理增强
- Model-aware runner 支持
- 上下文压缩和 image pre-analysis

**Feishu 集成**
- Thread binding 管理器（subagent 生命周期）
- Outbound message hooks，支持 media/image 发送
- Channel binding 支持
- Native reply dispatcher（streaming cards、thinking panels、tool status）
- Mention 标准化、quote 消息富化、recall 处理

**Gateway & Core**
- Node override policy 加固
- MCP HTTP bridge
- Session archive 改进
- Plugin infrastructure 加固

**Auto-reply**
- Tool call ID 透传
- Payload 去重

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CLI streaming 功能正常（日志可见、事件正确）
- [ ] Feishu thread binding 能正确关联会话
- [ ] Feishu 消息发送（文本、图片、media）功能正常
- [ ] Context overflow 保护触发阈值正确
- [ ] Model-aware runner 在 agents 中可用
- [ ] Gateway MCP bridge 响应正常

## 测试方法

1. 运行单元测试：`npm test`
2. Feishu 集成测试：在测试群中验证消息收发、thread 关联
3. CLI 测试：`openclaw agent start` 后验证 streaming 日志
4. Gateway 测试：`openclaw gateway start` 后验证 MCP bridge

## Co-Authored-By

items.author: dependabot[bot]

items.title: fix(tools): use alsoAllow instead of allow for Composio tool registration
items.content:
## Summary

- OpenClaw 2026.4+ changed `tools.allow` from additive to a **strict whitelist** — only listed tools are available, everything else is blocked.
- Our `syncAllowedComposioTools()` was writing Composio MCP tool names into `tools.allow`, which inadvertently blocked all core tools (exec, read, write, filesystem, apply_patch) from agent webchat sessions.
- Switches to `tools.alsoAllow` (additive on top of the profile) so Composio tools are registered **alongside** the `full` profile's core tools, not instead of them.
- Includes automatic migration: any stale `tools.allow` entries are moved to `alsoAllow` on next index rebuild.

## Test plan

- [ ] Start a new webchat session and verify exec/read/write/filesystem tools are available
- [ ] Verify Composio tools (Gmail, GitHub, etc.) still work
- [ ] Connect a new Composio app and confirm the tool index rebuild doesn't regress back to `tools.allow`


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

  • Providers: add bundled Qwen, Fireworks AI, and StepFun prov

items.author: artemgetmann

items.title: feat(message-bridge): 限制单进程重复初始化并支持 stop 重置
items.content:
# PR 说明

## 变更摘要

本次改动在 `message-bridge` runtime singleton 中引入显式初始化状态机，确保同一进程内不会通过插件入口重复初始化；并支持通过 `stopRuntime()` 重置门禁后再次初始化。同步补充集成测试与方案归档文档。

## 为什么要改

在 OpenCode 多工作区场景下，不同工作区实例会重复触发插件入口，连接失败或已初始化后仍可能出现重复初始化尝试与噪音日志，影响稳定性与排障效率。

## 影响范围 / 受影响工作区

- [x] `plugins/message-bridge`
- [ ] `plugins/message-bridge-openclaw`
- [ ] `packages/test-support`
- [ ] `integration/opencode-cui`
- [ ] `.github`
- [x] `docs`
- [ ] 其他（请补充）

## 验证证据

```bash
pnpm --filter @wecode/skill-opencode-plugin test:integration -- tests/integration/plugin.test.mjs
```

结果摘要：

- 62 passed, 0 failed
- 关键覆盖：
  - 首次失败后阻断重复初始化
  - 首次成功后阻断重复初始化
  - `stopRuntime()` 可重置并允许再次初始化
  - 并发首次调用共享同一个 initializing promise

未执行项与原因：

- 无

## 行为 / 契约验证

- 当前行为：singleton 初始化失败后可在后续入口再次尝试；成功后 stop 再入口仍可能触发新初始化。
- 改后行为：首次初始化尝试后（成功/失败）会阻断通过插件入口的重复初始化；调用 `stopRuntime()` 后重置门禁，可再次初始化。
- 本次明确改变了什么：
  - `singleton.ts` 引入 `never | initializing | succeeded | failed_latched` 状态机
  - 统一阻断错误与日志：`runtime_init_blocked_after_first_attempt` / `runtime.singleton.init_blocked_after_first_attempt`
  - `stopRuntime()` 重置门禁状态
- 本次明确没有改变什么：
  - gateway 协议与 action 语义未改
  - `message-bridge-openclaw` 与 `packages/test-support` 未受影响
- 对应验证证据：
  - `plugins/message-bridge/tests/integration/plugin.test.mjs`
  - `pnpm --filter @wecode/skill-opencode-plugin test:integration -- tests/integration/plugin.test.mjs`

## 风险与回滚关注点

- 默认值 / 兼容性 / 迁移影响：无
- 影响对象：同进程内通过插件入口触发 runtime 初始化的路径
- 回滚或止损方式：回滚本 PR 或临时恢复旧版 `singleton.ts` 逻辑（取消状态机门禁）

## 关联 Issue

- Closes #

## 提交前检查

- [x] 我已确认本仓库默认目标分支是 `main`
- [x] 我已使用仓库模板补全必要信息
- [x] 我已明确列出受影响工作区
- [x] 我已提供验证证据，或明确说明未执行原因
- [x] 我已说明受影响行为 / 契约及其验证方式
- [x] 我已确认 PR 描述与实际改动一致，并已写清当前行为、改后行为和关键变化点
- [x] 我已将主要行为变化与对应验证证据逐项对齐
- [x] 我已说明是否存在默认值变化、兼容性变化或迁移影响
- [x] 我已避免把无关工作树变更混入这次 PR

items.author: fuller-stack-dev

items.title: feat: Add Amazon Polly TTS provider
items.content:
## Summary

Adds Amazon Polly as a TTS (text-to-speech) provider for OpenClaw, enabling high-quality speech synthesis using AWS infrastructure with IAM role authentication.

## Changes

- **New TTS provider**: Amazon Polly integration as an OpenClaw extension
- **Generative engine support**: Uses Polly's latest generative voices (Pedro, Lupe for es-US; Andrés, Mia for es-MX)
- **WhatsApp compatible**: Outputs Opus-in-OGG format required by WhatsApp voice messages
- **IAM auth**: Uses boto3 credential chain (IAM roles, env vars, profiles) — no API keys in config
- **Configuration**: Standard OpenClaw TTS config in `messages.tts.providers.amazon-polly`

## Configuration Example

```json
{
  "messages": {
    "tts": {
      "provider": "amazon-polly",
      "providers": {
        "amazon-polly": {
          "enabled": true,
          "voice": "Pedro",
          "engine": "generative",
          "region": "us-east-1",
          "languageCode": "es-US"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Motivation

- Zero per-request API key management (IAM roles)
- Low latency from AWS regions
- Generative engine produces natural-sounding speech
- Cost effective (~$4/1M chars for generative)

## Testing

Tested on Ubuntu ARM64 (t4g.2xlarge) with WhatsApp channel. Voice messages delivered correctly in Opus/OGG format.
items.author: ArtemioPadilla

items.title: fix(github-copilot): return IDE identity headers from prepareRuntimeAuth
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes #58056 — GitHub Copilot provider returns HTTP 400 `missing Editor-Version header for IDE auth` on all inference requests.

The prior fix in #60755 added IDE identity headers (`Editor-Version`, `User-Agent`) to openclaw's transport layer (`copilot-dynamic-headers.ts`) and the extension's `wrapStreamFn`. However, **these code paths are bypassed during normal inference** because:

1. **Boundary-aware transport is never activated.** `pi-coding-agent` wraps `streamSimple` in a custom auth-injecting function during `createAgentSession`. The identity check in `stream-resolution.ts:116` (`currentStreamFn === streamSimple`) fails because it's a different function reference, so openclaw's transport layer — which has the correct headers — is never used.

2. **The actual inference path falls through to `@mariozechner/pi-ai`'s internal providers** (`anthropic.js`, `openai-responses.js`, `openai-completions.js`), which call pi-ai's own `buildCopilotDynamicHeaders` from `dist/providers/github-copilot-headers.js`. This is a **separate copy** that was never updated with the IDE headers — it only returns `X-Initiator`, `Openai-Intent`, and `Copilot-Vision-Request`.

3. **The extension's `wrapStreamFn` only covers `anthropic-messages` API** (`stream.ts:16`), leaving GPT-through-Copilot models (`openai-responses` API) with no IDE header injection at all.

## Fix

Return the IDE headers from `prepareRuntimeAuth` via the existing `ProviderPreparedRuntimeAuth.request.headers` contract. This is a one-line addition:

```typescript
request: { headers: buildCopilotIdeHeaders() },
```

These headers flow through `applyPreparedRuntimeRequestOverrides` → `resolveProviderRequestConfig` → `model.headers`. Pi-ai's providers read `model.headers` early in their `mergeHeaders` chain, so the IDE headers reach the outgoing HTTP request **regardless of which stream function is active**.

This fixes:
- `anthropic-messages` API (Claude models through Copilot)
- `openai-responses` API (GPT
items.author: noahbclarkson

items.title: OpenClaw plugin tarball still requires @ghostwater/vault-engine at runtime
items.content:
## Problem

The vault-engine OpenClaw plugin can now be configured and discovered, but the installed extension still fails to initialize at runtime.

Observed live failure from gateway logs:

```text
[plugins] vault-engine failed to load from /home/miner/.openclaw/extensions/vault-engine/dist/plugin.js:
Error: Cannot find module '@ghostwater/vault-engine'
Require stack:
- /home/miner/.openclaw/extensions/vault-engine/dist/runtime.js
```

This happens after the config/installability fixes landed. The remaining blocker is that the packed plugin artifact is not actually self-contained at runtime.

## What we learned

There were several earlier failures on the path here, but the current live blocker is specific:

1. We fixed the invalid installer config shape (`plugins.entries.vault-engine.package` was bogus).
2. We fixed the tarball installability problem caused by `workspace:*` when OpenClaw installs plugins outside the monorepo.
3. We can now get the plugin discovered/loaded enough for OpenClaw to see it.
4. But runtime still crashes because `dist/runtime.js` contains a live import of `@ghostwater/vault-engine`.

Current source evidence from the repo/build output:
- `packages/openclaw-plugin/build.mjs` claims it bundles `@ghostwater/vault-engine`
- but built output in `packages/openclaw-plugin/dist/runtime.js` still starts with:
  ```js
  import { query, rebuildIndex } from '@ghostwater/vault-engine';
  ```
- so the final packed plugin artifact still depends on monorepo-only runtime resolution

## Why this is a real bug

OpenClaw installs plugin artifacts into `~/.openclaw/extensions/...` and runs them outside the vault-engine monorepo. In that environment, `@ghostwater/vault-engine` is not present unless we either:
- truly bundle it into the plugin dist output, or
- ship it as a normal installable runtime dependency in a way the extension install can resolve

Right now we do neither reliably.

## Requirements

- R0: Installed OpenClaw plugin must initialize successf
items.author: unboxed-ai

items.title: Fix OpenClaw local link installs for headroom plugin
items.content:
## Summary
- make local linked installs work from `plugins/openclaw`, `plugins/openclaw/dist`, and repo-root `./plugins/openclaw`
- generate a self-contained `dist` plugin package during build so `--link .` from `dist` installs as a plugin
- add a no-op hook shim so valid plugin source paths also satisfy OpenClaw's hook-pack fallback validation instead of emitting `package.json missing openclaw.hooks`
- declare the plugin as a `context-engine` so a clean plugin install selects `plugins.slots.contextEngine = "headroom"`

## Why
OpenClaw 2026.4.5 currently falls back from local plugin install to hook-pack install when plugin install fails. That caused two problems for this plugin:
- `plugins/openclaw/dist` was not a valid plugin root, so `--link .` from `dist` failed plugin validation
- once the path became a valid plugin source, duplicate installs still printed a misleading hook-pack warning because the fallback path required `openclaw.hooks`

This change makes both plugin roots valid and makes the fallback path non-misleading, while keeping the shim hook as a no-op.

## Verified
- `npm run build`
- `npm test`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `openclaw plugins install --dangerously-force-unsafe-install --link .` from `plugins/openclaw`
- `openclaw plugins install --dangerously-force-unsafe-install --link .` from `plugins/openclaw/dist`
- `openclaw plugins install --dangerously-force-unsafe-install --link ./plugins/openclaw` from repo root
- repeated installs no longer emit `package.json missing openclaw.hooks`
- clean plugin install auto-selects `plugins.slots.contextEngine = "headroom"`

## Real-world validation notes
On a real `~/.openclaw` state, install only failed to select the engine when a stale `~/.openclaw/extensions/headroom` directory caused plugin install to fail first. In that case OpenClaw fell through to hook-pack linking, which correctly does not set plugin slots.

After removing the stale extension directory and stale hook fallback records, the normal install

items.author: JayZhu03

items.title: 0.4.35 install can leave OpenClaw home half-converged until plugins update runs
items.content:
## Summary
On a real host upgrade to `@openclawbrain/cli@0.4.35`, running the documented/manual install lane can leave the OpenClaw home **half-converged**:

- global CLI / daemon side moves to `0.4.35`
- but the installed OpenClaw hook/plugin in the target home stays on the older version

In my case, the host stayed split until I explicitly ran:

```bash
openclaw plugins update openclawbrain
```

After that, restart + install/status/proof converged cleanly.

## Expected
The published install/upgrade lane should fully converge the target OpenClaw home onto the requested OpenClawBrain version, or fail loudly.

If the documented path is:

```bash
openclawbrain install --openclaw-home ~/.openclaw
openclaw gateway restart
openclawbrain status --openclaw-home ~/.openclaw --detailed
openclawbrain proof --openclaw-home ~/.openclaw
```

then following that path should not leave the installed hook/plugin behind on an older version.

## Actual
On a real CormorantAI host during the `0.4.35` upgrade:

1. I upgraded the global CLI to `0.4.35`
2. I ran `openclawbrain install --openclaw-home /Users/cormorantai/.openclaw-CormorantAI`
3. I restarted the gateway and checked status
4. The host was still split:
   - daemon/global CLI side: `0.4.35`
   - installed hook/plugin in the OpenClaw home: still older
5. `openclaw plugins inspect openclawbrain` still reported the older installed plugin version
6. Only after running:

```bash
openclaw --profile CormorantAI plugins update openclawbrain
```

   did the installed hook move to `0.4.35`
7. After that, restart + install/status/proof all aligned and `proof` returned `success_and_proven`

## Why this matters
This is a bad upgrade UX because the documented/manual lane can look successful while the host is still **split-path version skewed**.

That makes the operator think the upgrade is done when the daemon and installed hook are actually on different versions.

## Requested fix
One of these should happen reliably:

1. `openclawbrain inst

items.title: refactor: streamline Dench Cloud API key to single source of truth
items.content:
## Summary

- Makes `auth-profiles.json` the single source of truth for the Dench Cloud API key. Bootstrap reads/writes the key there first, then derives it into `openclaw.json` (provider config, TTS, Composio) for runtime consumers.
- Creates `extensions/shared/dench-auth.ts` — a shared module all extensions import from instead of each duplicating their own `resolveApiKey` / `resolveGatewayUrl` logic.
- Removes redundant per-plugin `apiKey` from `apollo-enrichment` and `exa-search` config entries and cleans up stale keys on existing installs.
- Fixes the bug where re-running `npx denchclaw bootstrap` updated the key in `openclaw.json` but left `auth-profiles.json` stale.

## Test plan

- [x] All 51 bootstrap-external unit tests pass
- [x] All 45 bootstrap-command integration tests pass
- [x] All 32 extension tests pass (including updated composio bridge tests)
- [x] No linter errors
- [ ] Manual: run `npx denchclaw bootstrap` with an existing install and verify both `openclaw.json` and `auth-profiles.json` have the same key
- [ ] Manual: verify apollo-enrichment and exa-search tools still register and work

items.title: Bug: startup banner reports compaction summarization model as (unconfigured) even though LCM uses configured summaryModel at runtime
items.content:
## Summary

  `lossless-claw` startup logs report:

  ```text
  [plugins] [lcm] Compaction summarization model: (unconfigured)
 ```


  but actual LCM compaction later uses the configured summary model successfully.

  This appears to be a startup-banner/config-loading bug, not a real summarizer-resolution bug.

  ## Environment

  - OpenClaw: 2026.4.5
  - lossless-claw: 0.6.2
  - OS: Linux
  - Gateway mode: local

  ## Config

  I configured summaryModel under:

  {
    "plugins": {
      "entries": {
        "lossless-claw": {
          "enabled": true,
          "config": {
            "summaryModel": "openai-codex/gpt-5.4",
            "expansionModel": "openai-codex/gpt-5.4"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

  ## Reproduce

  1. Configure plugins.entries.lossless-claw.config.summaryModel
  2. Restart the OpenClaw gateway
  3. Observe startup logs
  4. Trigger /compact

  ## Observed behavior

  At startup, journal shows:

  [plugins] [lcm] Plugin loaded (enabled=true, db=/home/stilrmy/.openclaw/lcm.db, threshold=0.75)
  [plugins] [lcm] Compaction summarization model: (unconfigured)

  But later, during actual compaction, the plugin clearly uses the configured model:

  [lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 144446 -> 139189 conversation=15 summary=...
  [lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 139189 -> 100479 conversation=15 summary=...
  [lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 100479 -> 61952 conversation=15 summary=...
  [lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 61952 -> 44751 conversation=15 summary=...
  [lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 44751 -> 27139 conversation=15 summary=...
  [lcm] summarizer timed out; provider=openai-codex; model=gpt-5.4; timeout=60000ms; error=[lcm] summarizer timeout after 60000ms (initial)
  [lcm] summarizer timed out; provider=openai-codex; model=gpt-5.4; source=fallback
  [lcm] LCM compaction condensed pass (normal): 27139 -> 19986 conversation=15 summary=...

  So runtime compaction is using openai-codex/gpt

items.author: varg1714

items.title: Proposal: Pluggable context engines — enabling LCM as a plugin (like OpenClaw's lossless-claw)
items.content:
## Summary

I built a **context engine plugin slot** for Hermes Agent and the first plugin that uses it: [hermes-lcm](https://github.com/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm) — Lossless Context Management as a standalone installable plugin.

- **PR:** #5700 (the context engine slot — 2 commits, small diff)
- **Plugin:** [stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm](https://github.com/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm)

## The Problem

Hermes has no way for plugins to replace the context compressor. The `ContextCompressor` is hardwired in `run_agent.py`. This means:

1. Anyone wanting LCM (or any custom compaction) must fork and patch core
2. PR #4033 does exactly this — 12 modules, 278 tests, all jammed into `agent/lcm/`
3. OpenClaw solved this cleanly with a `contextEngine` plugin slot and [lossless-claw](https://github.com/martian-engineering/lossless-claw) as a standalone plugin

## The Solution

Two things:

### 1. Context engine slot (PR #5700)

A `ContextEngine` ABC in `agent/context_engine.py` that makes the compressor swappable:

```python
class ContextEngine(ABC):
    def update_from_response(self, usage) -> None: ...
    def should_compress(self, prompt_tokens) -> bool: ...
    def compress(self, messages, current_tokens) -> list: ...
    # + optional: get_tool_schemas(), handle_tool_call(), on_session_start/end/reset, etc.
```

`ContextCompressor` becomes the default implementation. Plugins register a replacement via `ctx.register_context_engine(engine)`. No behavior change for existing users.

### 2. hermes-lcm plugin

LCM implemented as a plugin against that slot:

- Immutable SQLite message store (every message preserved verbatim)
- Summary DAG with depth-aware nodes (D0 minutes → D1 hours → D2 days)
- 3-level escalation (L1 detailed → L2 bullets → L3 deterministic truncate)
- Agent tools: `lcm_grep`, `lcm_describe`, `lcm_expand`
- Install: `cp -r hermes-lcm ~/.hermes/plugins/hermes-lcm`

Based on the [LCM paper](https://papers.voltropy.com/LCM) by Ehrlich & Blackman (Voltropy, F
items.author: stephenschoettler

items.title: feat: pluggable context engine slot (enabling LCM and custom compaction)
items.content:
## What

Adds a **context engine plugin slot** so the built-in `ContextCompressor` can be replaced by third-party engines via the plugin system — the same pattern OpenClaw uses with its `contextEngine` slot.

This is the foundation for [Lossless Context Management (LCM)](https://papers.voltropy.com/LCM) as a plugin, without patching core.

## Why

- The current `ContextCompressor` is hardwired — there is no way for a plugin to replace compaction logic
- LCM (and future context engines) need to control what stays in the active window
- PR #4033 patches LCM directly into core (12 modules under `agent/lcm/`). A plugin slot is cleaner: small core change, engine logic lives in its own repo
- Mirrors how OpenClaw handles this with `lossless-claw`

## Changes

### Commit 1: ContextEngine ABC (`agent/context_engine.py`)

New abstract base class defining the pluggable interface:

**Required:** `name`, `update_from_response()`, `should_compress()`, `compress()`

**Optional (with defaults):** `on_session_start()`, `on_session_end()`, `on_session_reset()`, `get_tool_schemas()`, `handle_tool_call()`, `get_status()`, `update_model()`

`ContextCompressor` now inherits from `ContextEngine` as the default implementation. **Zero behavior change.**

### Commit 2: Plugin slot wiring

- `PluginContext.register_context_engine(engine)` — plugins call this in `register(ctx)`
- `PluginManager._context_engine` — stores the registered engine (single engine rule, like memory providers)
- `run_agent.py` — checks for a plugin engine at init, falls back to `ContextCompressor`
- `reset_session_state()` — uses `engine.on_session_reset()` instead of poking internal attributes

## Tests

- 19 new tests: ABC contract, defaults, plugin slot registration, rejection of duplicates/non-engines, compressor reset
- All 34 existing compressor tests pass unchanged

## Companion

The first plugin built against this slot: [hermes-lcm](https://github.com/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm) — Lossless Context Management

items.title: feat: unified error handling, invalidKey fast-fail, SKILL.md, and README expansion
items.content:
## Goal

Complete Slice 6 of the OpenClaw integration — unified error handling across all 21 tools, `invalidKey` fast-fail mechanism, OpenClaw SKILL.md behavioral guidance, and comprehensive README.

## Scope

### New Files (3)
- **`src/errors.ts`** — Shared utilities: `formatToolError()`, `successResult()`, `errorResult()`
- **`tests/errors.test.ts`** — 21 tests covering all error mapping branches
- **`SKILL.md`** — OpenClaw Skill file (behavioral guidance for agents per OCI-10)

### Modified Files (12)
- **`src/api/client.ts`** — `_invalidKey` flag (set on first 401, fast-fails subsequent requests), `invalidKey` getter, `configBaseUrl` getter
- **`src/tools/groups.ts`** — Removed local `successResult`/`errorResult`, imports from `../errors.js`, all 7 catch blocks use `formatToolError`
- **`src/tools/messages.ts`** — Same dedup + error mapping (4 catch blocks)
- **`src/tools/direct-messages.ts`** — Same dedup + error mapping (2 catch blocks)
- **`src/tools/delegations.ts`** — Same dedup + error mapping (8 catch blocks)
- **`tests/api/client.test.ts`** — 6 new tests: `invalidKey` fast-fail (4) + `configBaseUrl` getter (2)
- **`tests/tools/groups.test.ts`** — Mock API updated with `invalidKey`/`configBaseUrl`, error assertions updated to "Meshimize:" prefix
- **`tests/tools/messages.test.ts`** — Same mock/assertion updates
- **`tests/tools/direct-messages.test.ts`** — Same mock/assertion updates
- **`tests/tools/delegations.test.ts`** — Same mock/assertion updates
- **`README.md`** — Configuration, usage workflows, real-time features, error handling table, troubleshooting
- **`package.json`** — Added `SKILL.md` to `files` array

## Error Mapping Design

`formatToolError(error, baseUrl)` maps errors as follows:

| Error Type | Mapping |
|---|---|
| 401 Unauthorized | "Meshimize: Invalid or expired API key" (+ sets `_invalidKey` flag) |
| 429 Rate Limited | "Meshimize: Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later." |
| 500+ Server Error | "Meshimize: Server error. Pleas

items.title: fix(build): resolve @openclaw/plugin-sdk subpath imports in extensions/xai
items.content:
## Summary
- `pnpm check` fails on a fresh clone because `@openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` path mapping in `extensions/tsconfig.package-boundary.paths.json` only resolves to `dist/*.d.ts` files
- The `dist/` directory doesn't exist until `pnpm build` runs, so TypeScript cannot resolve subpath imports like `@openclaw/plugin-sdk/testing`, `@openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime`, and `@openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-web-search` used by `extensions/xai`

## Changes
- Add a source `.ts` fallback path (`../packages/plugin-sdk/src/*.ts`) to the `@openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` mapping
- TypeScript will first try the dist `.d.ts` files (for post-build), then fall back to source `.ts` files (for pre-build/fresh clone)

## Test plan
- [ ] Fresh clone + `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` + `pnpm check` should pass without needing `pnpm build` first
- [ ] Existing build workflows should be unaffected (dist path still takes priority)

Fixes #61893

items.title: feat(databricks): add native databricks serving provider with dynamic model parsing
items.content:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- **Problem**: OpenClaw lacked native support for Databricks Serving Models. Interacting with Databricks APIs via standard OpenAI proxy setups failed because Databricks requires strict and completely different request URL routing (`/serving-endpoints/<model>/invocations`), and rigidly rejects extraneous parameters (e.g., `store`, `service_tier`) that standard OpenAI completions send.
- **Why it matters**: Databricks users need a seamless, code-free onboarding experience directly through the `openclaw onboarding` CLI to easily map their deployed endpoints and securely use their active workspace directly.
- **What changed**:
  - Implemented a native `databricks` bundled provider plugin using `openclaw/plugin-sdk`.
  - Added dynamic model parsing logic in `catalog.run` that automatically lists active REST endpoints from the workspace's `/api/2.0/serving-endpoints` (filtering for `EXTERNAL_MODEL` and chat endpoints).
  - Used `wrapStreamFn` to dynamically intercept and rewrite internal routing URLs to `<baseUrl>/serving-endpoints/<model>/invocations`, ensuring compatibility with Databricks without touching the core orchestration logic.
  - Added automated payload sanitization to discard Databricks-intolerant OpenAI payload parameters.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: The core OpenClaw streaming, gateway protocol, and global orchestration loops were untouched. All behavior changes were confined internally to the new Databricks plugin boundary (`extensions/databricks/*`).

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

items.title: fix: declare all user-configurable env vars in openclaw manifests
items.content:
Fixes the ClawHub security scan (medium confidence, "suspicious").

The scanner flagged undeclared environment variables. These are optional overrides with sensible defaults: container names and OAuth token file paths, used in `kometa.sh`, `recyclarr.sh`, `unpackerr.sh`, `trakt.sh`, and `simkl.sh` but missing from `openclaw.plugin.json` and `SKILL.md`.

Added to both manifests:

- `KOMETA_CONTAINER`, `KOMETA_DOCKER_CMD`
- `RECYCLARR_CONTAINER`, `RECYCLARR_DOCKER_CMD`
- `UNPACKERR_CONTAINER`, `UNPACKERR_DOCKER_CMD`
- `TRAKT_TOKEN_FILE`, `SIMKL_TOKEN_FILE`

`docker` and `ssh` were already in `optionalBins` with gating notes. Every `ssh`/`docker` call is inside an `if [[ -n "$DOCKER_HOST_SSH" ]]` block, neither is called unconditionally.

Script env var usage and manifest declarations are now in sync.
