items.content:
# Hacker News AI 社区动态日报 2026-04-07

> 数据来源: [Hacker News](https://news.ycombinator.com/) | 共 30 条 | 生成时间: 2026-04-07 02:41 UTC

---

# Hacker News AI 社区动态日报 (2026-04-07)

## 1. 今日速览

今日 HN AI 社区的情绪呈现明显的**两极分化**。一方面，针对 **Anthropic** 及其开发工具（特别是 Claude Code）的**信任危机与强烈不满**占据了头条，开发者们对工具的可用性和公司的公关策略表达了极度失望。另一方面，**OpenAI** 发布的工业政策报告及 API 赠送活动则引导了关于 AI 时代经济结构与可持续性的讨论。此外，社区依然保持着对**底层架构与极客精神**的热爱，出现了一些高质量的非 LLM 类编程语言和硬核图形学项目。

---

## 2. 热门新闻与讨论

### 🔬 模型与研究

1.  **Screenshot of interesting generalization from a tiny corpus on linear RNN**
    *   **链接**: [HN 讨论](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47664632)
    *   **数据**: 分数 3 | 评论 0
    *   **关注理由**: 尽管互动较少，但在 Transformer 垄断的当下，关于 **Linear RNN（线性循环神经网络）** 小样本泛化能力的展示令人耳目一新，暗示了非 Transformer 架构在特定高效场景下的潜力。

2.  **Quantum computing could fix AI's sustainability problem**
    *   **链接**: [原文](https://www.japantimes.co.jp/commentary/2026/04/06/world/quantum-computing-ais-sustainability-problem/) | [HN 讨论](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47669803)
    *   **数据**: 分数 3 | 评论 1
    *   **关注理由**: 探讨了 AI 能耗问题的终极解法——量子计算。社区对此持审慎乐观态度，关注点在于量子计算何时能真正通过算法优化降低 AI 的巨量能耗。

### 🛠️ 工具与工程

1.  **Issue: Claude Code is unusable for complex engineering tasks with Feb updates**
    *   **链接**: [GitHub Issue](https://github​.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/42796) | [HN 讨论](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47660925)
    *   **数据**: 分数 779 | 评论 473
    *   **关注理由**: **今日最热话题**。开发者集体吐槽 Claude Code 在二月更新后性能断崖式下跌，难以处理复杂任务。这反映了目前 AI 辅助编程工具面临的核心痛点：**“智能”与“可控性”之间的平衡**，以及模型迭代导致的稳定性缺失。

2.  **Sky – an Elm-inspired language that compiles to Go**
    *   **链接**: [GitHub](https://github​.com/anzellai/sky) | [HN 讨论](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47662116)
    *   **数据**: 分数 133 | 评论 49
    *   **关注理由**: 开发者对 AI 生成的样板代码感到厌倦，开始回归**函数式语言与强类型**的融合。Sky 语言结合了 Elm 的易用性与 Go 的性能，为后端开发提供了一种 AI 难以轻易生成的高效抽象。

3.  **Show HN: Meta-agent: self-improving agent harnesses from live traces**
    *   **链接**: [GitHub](https://github​.com/canvas-org/meta-agent) | [HN

items.title: Telegram: add oversized-reply sendDocument fallback and update tests
items.content:
### Motivation
- `TelegramConnector.send()` could forward replies longer than Telegrams limit and silently drop them when the Bot API returned permanent errors. 
- The change restores explicit handling of Telegrams 4096-char limit and ensures oversized replies are delivered or fail loudly so caller health/error paths can react. 
- E2E/unit tests previously asserting chunking behavior needed updating to match the new single-reply delivery semantics.

### Description
- Add `_MAX_MSG_LEN = 4096` and make `send()` use `sendMessage` for messages `<= _MAX_MSG_LEN` and fall back to a single `sendDocument` call for oversized replies via a new `_send_document()` helper. 
- Change `_send_chunk()` to return `bool` and add failure propagation so callers can detect permanent failures, and raise `RuntimeError` when delivery and fallback both fail. 
- Implement multipart-safe document upload using `aiohttp.FormData` and a text attachment named `openclaw-response.txt` for oversized replies. 
- Update tests to reflect the new behavior by asserting `sendDocument` is used for long replies and `sendMessage` for short ones in `eldon/tests/test_telegram_connector.py` and `eldon/tests/test_telegram_e2e.py`, and adjust docs/comments accordingly (files changed: `eldon/src/openclaw/connectors/telegram.py`, `eldon/tests/test_telegram_connector.py`, `eldon/tests/test_telegram_e2e.py`).

### Testing
- Ran the targeted pytest suite with `cd eldon && PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_telegram_connector.py tests/test_telegram_e2e.py -q`, which completed with `59 passed, 4 warnings`.
- All modified unit and e2e assertions now pass and validate the document-fallback and short-message `sendMessage` paths.

------

items.title: [Feature]: Add GitHub Copilot as LLM provider
items.content:
### Surface

Providers / routing

### Problem statement

GitHub Copilot is GitHub's AI coding assistant. Currently users have to use OpenAI-compatible reverse proxy, like [copilot-api](https://github.com/ericc-ch/copilot-api), to use it as a provider in Loong.

For reference, here's a list of other claws that support GitHub Copilot (non-exhaustive):

- [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/providers/github-copilot.md)
- [PicoClaw](https://github.com/sipeed/picoclaw?tab=readme-ov-file#-providers-llm)
- [ZeroClaw](https://github.com/zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw/blob/master/docs/reference/api/providers-reference.md#provider-catalog)
- ...

### Proposed solution

It would be beneficial to add first-class support for GitHub Copilot as a provider. While it does not officially provide documentation on making LLM requests, there's community resources to do that.

We're using undocumented APIs, which GitHub could change or revoke at any time. So a warning might be helpful to state that "Using GitHub Copilot as provider might break without prior notice". See [comment in `copilot.rs` from ZeroClaw](https://github.com/zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw/blob/c1810193456667a471d5d105d138e0474e884889/src/providers/copilot.rs#L3-L12).

### Non-goals / out of scope

_No response_

### Alternatives considered

_No response_

### Acceptance criteria

- Users can use GitHub Copilot as provider directly, without additional setup

### Policy / security / breaking sensitivity

No, this looks routine

### Rollout / rollback notes

_No response_

### Impact

_No response_

### Submission checks

- [x] I searched existing issues and did not find the same request already tracked.
- [x] I used project-scoped wording and removed secrets or private data from examples.

items.title: Align Telegram Graph preflight with routing keywords
items.content:
### Motivation
- Prevent false "Microsoft Graph credentials are missing" blockers when requests are routed to operations but use router keywords not covered by the intake preflight.
- Ensure the intake capability gating logic for Outlook/Graph operations is consistent with the deterministic routing keywords used by the Telegram router.

### Description
- Exported `GRAPH_OPERATION_KEYWORDS` from `src/orchestration/lola/telegram-router.ts` and reused it in `src/orchestration/lola/telegram-intake.ts` so routing and preflight keywords are sourced from one place.
- Replaced duplicated substring checks in `evaluateTelegramIntake` with `GRAPH_OPERATION_KEYWORDS` and preserved support for the `M365_GRAPH_ACCESS_TOKEN` alias alongside `LOLA_M365_GRAPH_ACCESS_TOKEN` and client-credential fallbacks.
- Added a unit test `accepts follow-up operations when only M365_GRAPH_ACCESS_TOKEN alias is set` to `src/orchestration/lola/telegram-intake.test.ts` that verifies follow-up/operations requests pass when the alias token is present.
- Modified and committed the three files: `src/orchestration/lola/telegram-router.ts`, `src/orchestration/lola/telegram-intake.ts`, and `src/orchestration/lola/telegram-intake.test.ts`.

### Testing
- Ran `pnpm test src/orchestration/lola/telegram-intake.test.ts`, which executed 15 tests and all passed.
- Ran the repository lint/format hooks via `scripts/committer` as part of the commit and the hooks completed successfully.

items.title: Change tool call format to XML in templates
items.content:

items.author: Eldonlandsupply

items.title: [Feature Request]: Global model call rate limiting across all agents
items.content:
## Problem Description

Users with tiered API pricing plans (e.g., MiniMax Starter: 600 calls / 5 hours) need a way to limit the overall model call rate across **all active agents** to avoid triggering rate limits.

Currently, OpenClaw provides:
- `cron.maxConcurrentRuns`: Limits concurrent cron job runs
- `retry.backoffMs`: Passive retry delay after rate limit errors  
- Provider-level configurations

However, there is **no configurable global rate limiter** that enforces a maximum number of model API calls per minute across all agents (main, subagents, cron jobs, etc.).

## Use Case

A user with a 600 calls / 5 hours plan (≈ 2 calls/minute) wants to ensure that:
- All 3 scheduled cron jobs (02:00, 09:00, 18:00)
- All interactive sessions
- All subagent executions

...collectively do not exceed 2 model calls per minute.

## Expected Behavior

A configuration option like:

```json
{
  "models": {
    "globalRateLimit": {
      "maxCallsPerMinute": 2
    }
  }
}
```

Or alternatively at the provider level:

```json
{
  "providers": {
    "minimax": {
      "rateLimit": {
        "maxCallsPerMinute": 2
      }
    }
  }
}
```

When the limit is reached, subsequent calls should queue or wait until the next minute window.

## Workaround (Insufficient)

Currently users must:
1. Manually stagger task schedules to avoid overlapping
2. Rely on fallback chains to handle rate limit errors reactively
3. Use external rate limiting (e.g., a proxy or wrapper)

None of these provide true global rate control across all agents and sessions.

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: 2026.4.2 (latest available at time of report: 2026.4.5)
- Node.js: v22.22.2
- OS: Linux
- Model provider: MiniMax (also applicable to other tiered providers)

---

**Would appreciate a feature addition or guidance on how to achieve this with existing configuration options.**
items.author: zepoodd

items.title: fix: callAI auth fallback to models.json apiKey
items.content:
## Fix Summary

**Problem:** gtw /gtw new fails with 401 when using minimax because callAI only reads auth tokens from auth-profiles.json (access field), but minimax apiKey is stored in models.json providers.minimax.apiKey.

**Root Cause:** OpenClaw has fallback to read models.json apiKey, but gtw callAI did not.

**Fix:** 3-priority auth token lookup:
1. auth-profiles.json profiles[provider:default].access (PAT)
2. auth-profiles.json profiles[provider:default].key (api_key)
3. models.json providers[provider].apiKey (inline)

items.title: Cron: normalize jobId to id when loading jobs.json
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: Cron jobs stored with only `jobId` (no `id`) in `jobs.json` failed at runtime with `unknown cron job id` because the scheduler looked up jobs by `id` only. Doctor migration already fixed this on `doctor --fix`, but the gateway load path did not.
- Why it matters: Users editing the store or following docs that emphasize `jobId` expect stable identifiers to work without running doctor first.
- What changed: Shared `normalizeCronJobIdentityFields` runs when the cron service loads the store (and doctor migration reuses it) so `id` is set from `jobId` when needed and the legacy `jobId` key is dropped in memory.
- What did NOT change: On-disk files are not rewritten on load alone; the next explicit persist still writes the canonical shape.

- Closes #62246
- Related #62246
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The on-disk JSON shape allowed `jobId` without `id`; runtime lookup used `id` only, while doctor migration normalized the file separately.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Runtime load did not apply the same identity normalization as doctor.
- Contributing context: API and tools often refer to the stable name as `jobId` while persisted jobs use `id`.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test: `src/cron/normalize-job-identity.test.ts`, `src/cron/service/store.test.ts`
- Scenario: A job with only `jobId` in `jobs.json` loads and `findJobOrThrow` resolves the stable id; disk file unchanged until persist.

items.author: Saturno-1228

items.title: [Bug] Inbound image attachments not downloaded to media/inbound in BlueBubbles and Slack channels
items.content:
**Environment**
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.5
- OS: macOS 15.4 (arm64, Mac mini)
- Channels affected: BlueBubbles (iMessage), Slack
- BlueBubbles Private API: disabled

**Describe the bug**

When a user sends an image attachment via iMessage (BlueBubbles) or Slack, the agent receives the message and a filename reference, but the attachment file is never written to `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/`. As a result the agent cannot read or describe the image — there's nothing on disk to load.

The agent prompt contains a reference like `IMG_1127.png` or `attached://IMG_1127.png` but the file is absent from `media/inbound/`.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Send an iMessage or Slack message containing an image attachment to the agent
2. Agent receives the message but `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/` has no new file
3. Agent cannot describe or process the image

**Expected behaviour**

Inbound attachments should be fetched and cached to `media/inbound/` before the agent turn runs, consistent with how webchat image uploads work.

**Additional context**
- Webchat image drops work correctly end-to-end
- For BlueBubbles specifically: unclear whether attachment download requires Private API or only typing indicators/read receipts. If Private API is required for attachment download, this should be documented clearly
- Slack has no equivalent Private API requirement, so the Slack case appears to be a separate gap
- We attempted to patch `attached://` URI handling in the prompt image detector and tool validators as a workaround — those patches are in place but don't resolve the root issue since the file never lands on disk in the first place

**Workaround**

Webchat drag-and-drop works. No workaround available for iMessage or Slack surfaces.

Additional finding after further investigation:
Dug deeper into the source. There are actually two bugs:

Bug 1 (patched locally): downloadBlueBubblesAttachment() was called without allowPrivateNetwork in its options. Since BlueBubbles runs on a LAN IP, the SSRF pol

items.author: sinkers

items.title: Cron store: `jobId` in jobs.json not normalized to `id`, causes "unknown cron job id"
items.content:
## Summary

When cron jobs are stored in `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json` with the **`jobId`** field (consistent with docs that describe `jobId` as the canonical identifier for Gateway/API tool calls), the scheduler fails to resolve those jobs at runtime.

## Observed error

```
Cron failed: Error: unknown cron job id: <stable-job-slug>
```

The stable slug matches the string you put in `jobId`, but the in-memory lookup uses **`job.id`** only.

## Why it happens (current behavior)

- `findJobOrThrow` matches with `state.store.jobs.find((j) => j.id === id)`.
- Jobs created via the normal `cron.add` path get an `id` (e.g. UUID).
- Hand-edited or migrated JSON that only has **`jobId`** never gets an `id`, so lookups by the documented stable name fail.

## Expected behavior (one of)

1. **Load-time migration:** In `ensureLoaded` (or equivalent), if `jobId` is present and `id` is missing, set `id` from `jobId` (and optionally drop `jobId` on persist for a single canonical shape), **or**
2. **Lookup:** Resolve jobs by `id` **or** `jobId` consistently, **or**
3. **Docs:** Explicitly state that the on-disk store must use **`id`** (not `jobId`) and that `jobId` is only for RPC/tool payloads—if that is intentional.

## Minimal reproduction idea

1. Stop the gateway.
2. Add a job object to `jobs.json` with `"jobId": "repro-stable-id"` and **no** `"id"`, valid `schedule`, `sessionTarget`, `payload`, etc.
3. Start the gateway; trigger a wake/run that references `repro-stable-id`.
4. Observe `unknown cron job id: repro-stable-id`.

## Environment

- Observed on packaged OpenClaw (cron scheduler / gateway), version in the `2026.2.x` line; likely applies to any build sharing the same store + lookup logic.

---

Happy to help test a patch or adjust the issue if maintainers prefer a specific direction (migration vs dual-key lookup vs doc-only).
items.author: cesarfavero

items.title: npm install -g openclaw still missing bundled extension dependencies (regression from #52719)
items.content:
## Summary

#52719 was closed as fixed, but the npm distribution (`npm install -g openclaw@latest`) still does not include channel extension dependencies. The CLI crashes on startup with `Cannot find module` errors, and `openclaw update` cannot recover because it runs `npm install -g` first (which wipes any manually-installed deps), then tries to run `openclaw doctor` — which crashes for the same reason.

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.4.5 (3e72c03)
- Node v22.22.1
- Installed via `npm install -g openclaw` inside Docker (`node:22-slim`)

## Reproduction

\`\`\`bash
# Fresh install
npm install -g openclaw

# Immediate crash
openclaw gateway
# [openclaw] Failed to start CLI: Error: Cannot find module '@buape/carbon'

# openclaw update also crashes
openclaw update
# ✓ global update (123.52s)
# ✗ openclaw doctor (4.1s)
#     Error: Cannot find module '@buape/carbon'
\`\`\`

## Missing packages

These are imported at runtime but not declared in `package.json`:

| Channel | Missing packages |
|---------|-----------------|
| Telegram | `grammy`, `@grammyjs/runner`, `@grammyjs/transformer-throttler`, `@grammyjs/types` |
| Discord | `@buape/carbon` |
| Feishu/Lark | `@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk` |
| Slack | `@slack/web-api` |

Verified by inspecting `package.json` — none of these appear in `dependencies`, `peerDependencies`, or `optionalDependencies`.

## Self-breaking update cycle

`openclaw update` cannot fix this because:
1. It runs `npm install -g openclaw` → wipes any manually-installed extension deps
2. It then runs `openclaw doctor` → CLI crashes because deps are gone
3. User must manually run `npm install grammy @buape/carbon ...` to recover

This means there is no safe self-service upgrade path without maintaining a hardcoded list of undeclared dependencies.

## Suggested fix

Add these packages to `optionalDependencies` in `package.json`. This way:
- `npm install -g openclaw` installs them automatically
- Users who don't need certain channels aren't blocked if a dep f

items.title: fix(net): strip external undici dispatcher from custom fetchImpl calls
items.content:
## Summary

- Fixes Slack file attachment downloads silently failing on OpenClaw 2026.4.5 with `InvalidArgumentError: invalid onRequestStart method`
- Root cause: `fetchWithSsrFGuard` passes an external undici dispatcher (from the bundled `undici` package) through `init` to custom `fetchImpl` callers. These custom fetchers (e.g. Slack/Telegram media) wrap Node's built-in `fetch()`, which bundles its own undici 8.x internally. The handler interface changed between undici 7→8, causing the version mismatch error.
- Fix: strip the `dispatcher` property from `init` before forwarding to custom `fetchImpl` callers. The SSRF DNS-pinning validation has already been performed before the custom fetcher is invoked, so security guarantees are preserved.

## Test plan

- [ ] Existing `fetch-guard.ssrf.test.ts` tests pass (no behavior change for runtime-fetch path)
- [ ] Slack file attachment download works on Node 22+ with undici 8.x
- [ ] Verify SSRF protection still blocks private IPs when custom fetchImpl is used

Fixes #62218

items.title: Harden codex_workflow preflight checks, require `timeout`, and make validator type-safe
items.content:
### Motivation
- Prevent non-deterministic failures when `git` is missing by ensuring dependency checks run before any `git` invocation. 
- Ensure runtime behavior of `run_workflow.sh` (which uses `timeout`) is reflected in the declared contract so preflight validates the runtime environment. 
- Avoid uncaught exceptions in the validator when top-level config sections are malformed so validation stays fail-loud and structured. 

### Description
- Move required-binaries `command -v` checks in `codex_workflow/preflight.sh` to run immediately after emitting the runtime contract and before any `git` calls. 
- Add `timeout` to `codex_workflow/config.yaml` and require it in the JSON schema via a `contains` rule in `codex_workflow/config.schema.json`. 
- In `codex_workflow/validate_inputs.py` add explicit `isinstance(..., dict)` checks for top-level sections to avoid `AttributeError` when a section is the wrong type. 
- Require presence of `timeout` in `dependencies.required_binaries` validation and add a top-level `except Exception` handler in `main()` to preserve `[ERROR] VALIDATION_FAILED` output for unexpected exceptions. 

### Testing
- Ran `python3 codex_workflow/validate_inputs.py --config codex_workflow/config.yaml --schema codex_workflow/config.schema.json --emit-runtime codex_workflow/out/runtime.contract.json`, which completed successfully. 
- Ran `./codex_workflow/preflight.sh codex_workflow/config.yaml codex_workflow/out/runtime.contract.json`, which returned `[OK] preflight complete`. 
- Executed `./codex_workflow/run_workflow.sh codex_workflow/config.yaml`, which ran the workflow end-to-end and returned `[OK] workflow complete`. 
- Ran repository lint/format tooling via the committer flow which reported zero linter warnings/errors on the modified files.

------

items.title: Harden codex_workflow runner execution and config validation
items.content:
### Motivation
- Eliminate a command-injection risk where `CONFIG_PATH` was interpolated into shell commands and executed via `bash -lc`, which also broke legitimate paths with spaces. 
- Ensure a structured `status.json` is always emitted on exit so failed runs produce machine-readable status instead of nothing. 
- Honor the runtime-configured `execution.steps` contract (enabled, retry_count, timeout, command) instead of running hardcoded commands. 
- Prevent YAML inline-comment parsing bugs that could silently disable preflight checks and enforce schema `additionalProperties: false` semantics.

### Description
- Reworked `codex_workflow/run_workflow.sh` to add an `on_exit` trap that always writes `codex_workflow/out/status.json` with `result`, `stage`, `message`, and `exit_code`. 
- Added `run_argv_step` and `run_command_step` helpers in `run_workflow.sh` so preflight and validation are invoked argv-style (no unquoted interpolation of `CONFIG_PATH`), and runtime-defined steps are executed with retries, timeouts, and per-attempt stage tracking. 
- Replaced ad-hoc `bash -lc` invocation for orchestrator-controlled steps with safe argv execution and limited `bash -lc` only for timed command evaluation via `timeout` when running configured step strings. 
- Hardened `codex_workflow/validate_inputs.py` YAML parsing by stripping inline comments safely (quote-aware), added `reject_unknown_keys` checks to implement `additionalProperties: false` behavior at root and nested sections, and added shape checks for sections and per-step unknown-key rejection. 

### Testing
- Ran `python3 codex_workflow/validate_inputs.py --config codex_workflow/config.yaml --schema codex_workflow/config.schema.json --emit-runtime codex_workflow/out/runtime.contract.json`, which completed successfully. 
- Ran `./codex_workflow/run_workflow.sh codex_workflow/config.yaml`, which completed successfully and produced expected verification artifacts. 
- Ran failure path: `./codex_workflow/run_workflow.sh
items.author: Eldonlandsupply

items.title: Align CostClaw KPIs to sessions with usage payloads
items.content:
### Motivation
- Per-task KPIs in the CostClaw view used `sessions.length` and treated null `usage` as successful, creating a denominator mismatch versus workflow metrics that only aggregate sessions with `usage`.
- This mismatch systematically biased metrics like cost-per-successful-task, tool-calls-per-task, and retry rates when some sessions lacked `usage` payloads.

### Description
- Scope per-task denominators to usage-backed sessions by adding `const sessionsWithUsage = sessions.filter((s) => Boolean(s.usage));` in `buildCostClawSnapshots` in `ui/src/ui/views/usage.ts`.
- Base `successfulTasks`, `totalTasks`, and `totalToolCalls` on `sessionsWithUsage` instead of all `sessions`, and update `regressions_introduced` to count only sessions that include `usage`.
- Add a visible primary KPI `sessions_missing_usage` to report how many sessions were excluded from workflow-derived KPIs.

### Testing
- Ran typecheck/build step with `pnpm tsgo`, which completed successfully with only a local Node engine warning about the runtime version.
- Ran repository lint/format hooks via the committer script (`scripts/committer`), which executed `oxlint`/`oxfmt` and completed without warnings or errors.

------

items.author: Eldonlandsupply

items.title: Fix parcels validator config and enforce required_fields in GeoJSON validator
items.content:
### Motivation

- `schemas/parcels.schema.json` advertised a JSON Schema draft but used a custom shape, which misleads consumers and could break schema-driven workflows.  
- `scripts/validate_geojson.py` only checked `id` and ignored any `required_fields` declared by the schema/config, allowing required fields to go unenforced.  
- The change prefers an explicit custom-config approach for the parcels validator to preserve the lightweight validator contract while avoiding false JSON Schema claims.

### Description

- Convert `schemas/parcels.schema.json` into an explicit validator config by removing the `$schema` declaration, adding `geojson_type`, and keeping `required_fields` as the validator contract.  
- Update `scripts/validate_geojson.py` so `validate_feature()` accepts `required_fields` and enforces every required field, reporting missing or empty values with feature-index/id tagging.  
- Add validation for the `required_fields` config to ensure it is a non-empty `list[str]` and emit a schema error when it is invalid, and make the root type check use `geojson_type` from the config.  
- Update `docs/runbooks/codex-task-pickup.md` and `docs/architecture/orchestration-framework.md` to document that `schemas/parcels.schema.json` is a custom validator config consumed by `scripts/validate_geojson.py`.

### Testing

- Ran `python3 -m py_compile scripts/validate_geojson.py` which passed with no syntax errors.  
- Executed `python3 scripts/validate_geojson.py --input <tmp>/input.geojson --schema <tmp>/schema.json` with `required_fields: ["id","county"]` and confirmed missing/empty required fields produced the expected validation errors and nonzero exit.  
- Executed the validator with an invalid `required_fields` type (e.g., a string) and confirmed the script emitted the schema error `schema required_fields must be a non-empty list of non-empty strings` and returned a failing exit code.

------

The first governance ontology: 5 meta-rules + 12 invariants across 6 dimensions, born from the 2026-04-07 adversarial audit that found 22 declaration-vs-reality gaps.

Structure:
- Meta-concepts: Declaration, Enforcement, Verification, Gap
- 5 Meta-rules (MR-1..MR-5): the rules about rules MR-1: every declaration must have enforcement code MR-2: every enforcement must have verification MR-3: declaration changes must propagate to all layers MR-4: silent failure is a bug MR-5: health fields must have freshness guarantees
- 12 Invariants (INV-*): concrete declaration-enforcement-verification triples covering tools, cron, notifications, env vars, health, deployment
- Self-audit: meta_audit section acknowledges 5 dimensions NOT yet covered

governance_checker.py: reads the ontology, auto-verifies invariants by scanning code for enforcement patterns and test names. Produces structured report: 7 pass / 5 known gaps / 0 fail.

Key insight: the ontology's value is not the 12 checks it contains, but the meta-rules that make MISSING checks visible. When a new declaration is added without enforcement, the structure itself reveals the gap.

items.title: Simplify deployment: npm package, setup wizard, and Cloudflare Access auth
items.content:
## Summary

Deploying Agent Cockpit today requires cloning a repo, manually editing `.env`, creating OAuth apps in Google Cloud Console / GitHub, setting up Cloudflare tunnels by hand, and configuring PM2. This is a multi-step developer workflow, not a product install experience.

This issue proposes three changes that work together to make deployment a one-command experience on both macOS and Windows:

1. **Publish to npm** — install via `npm install -g agent-cockpit`
2. **Interactive setup wizard** — first-run CLI walkthrough that generates all configuration
3. **Cloudflare Access auth as the primary auth mode** — eliminate OAuth app creation entirely for remote users, with a simple password fallback for local-only use

---

## Current Pain Points

| Step | Friction |
|------|----------|
| Installation | Clone a git repo, run npm install — requires git knowledge |
| Auth setup | Create a Google Cloud project, configure OAuth credentials, set callback URLs — 15-minute detour |
| Environment config | Manually copy .env.example, edit values, generate SESSION_SECRET |
| Cloudflare tunnel | 5 separate CLI commands, manual YAML config file creation |
| Process management | Install PM2 globally, configure startup scripts |
| Windows support | ONBOARDING.md uses \`brew install\` everywhere — no Windows path |

---

## Design

### Part 1: Publish to npm

**Goal:** `npm install -g agent-cockpit && agent-cockpit` replaces clone + npm install.

#### Package structure

The npm package should include all runtime files (compiled or run via tsx as today). The `bin` field in `package.json` provides the CLI entry point:

```json
{
  "name": "agent-cockpit",
  "bin": {
    "agent-cockpit": "./bin/cli.js"
  }
}
```

#### CLI entry point (`bin/cli.js`)

A thin wrapper that:
1. Checks if this is a first run (no config exists) — if so, launches the setup wizard (Part 2)
2. Otherwise, starts the server (equivalent to today's `npm start`)

#### Data directory

When installed as a global n

[RemoteOpenClaw](https://remoteopenclaw.com) is an open marketplace for discovering, sharing, and distributing AI skills and personas built on [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw).

items.author: dependabot[bot]

items.title: macOS permissions are confusing because desktop control spans multiple process identities
items.content:
## Summary

On macOS, the current permissions experience for desktop control is confusing because Clawd Cursor operations appear to span multiple process identities instead of one clear app identity.

Users expect to grant access once to "Clawd Cursor" and be done. In practice, they may need to enable permissions for a mix of Terminal, node, OpenClaw, OpenClaw Gateway, AE server, or other helpers, and it is not obvious which one is actually responsible for the failing operation.

## What is happening

macOS grants **Screen Recording** and **Accessibility** permissions per app/process identity, not per project folder or codebase.

In the current setup, desktop control may run through a combination of:
- Terminal or iTerm
- node
- OpenClaw / OpenClaw Gateway style background services
- helper processes

That means a user can grant access to one visible host, but the actual screen or control action may be happening in another process, so the product still fails with a permission error.

## Why this is a problem

From the user perspective, this feels arbitrary and broken:
- they enable Terminal, but screen capture still fails
- they are not told clearly which app entry to enable
- they may need a full restart, but the product does not make that obvious
- the result is a frustrating first-run experience even when the underlying automation works

## Suggested short-term improvements

At minimum, the CLI should:
1. Detect the actual host process chain involved in desktop control
2. Print the exact macOS app names the user should enable
3. Clearly distinguish between:
   - **Accessibility** for clicking, typing, control
   - **Screen Recording** for visual perception
4. Tell the user when a full restart is required
5. Avoid vague messaging like "grant permission" without naming the specific host apps/processes

## Suggested long-term direction

The clean fix is architectural: move desktop automation behind a single macOS app identity, for example **Clawd Cursor.app**.

Reco

items.title: Fix YAML formatting and improve hook daemon error handling
items.content:
## Summary
This PR addresses YAML formatting inconsistencies, improves error handling in the hook daemon, reorganizes Langfuse secret management, and refactors Loki VPA configuration.

## Key Changes

### YAML Formatting
- Standardized indentation in `gemini-api-key.sops.yaml` and `telegram-bot-token.sops.yaml` from 4 spaces to 2 spaces for consistency with Kubernetes conventions

### Hook Daemon Error Handling
- Added new `DaemonHttpError` exception class to distinguish HTTP errors from other daemon failures
- Enhanced error reporting with detailed recovery instructions in `hook_dispatch.py`
- Improved error messages to guide users to daemon logs and recovery documentation
- Changed HTTP error handling to raise exceptions instead of silently returning `None`

### Langfuse Secret Management
- Removed `langfuse-api-keys-externalsecret.yaml` from both `litellm` and `ollama` namespaces
- Cleaned up TODO comments referencing the suspended Langfuse service
- Updated kustomization files to reflect removal of Langfuse secret resources

### Loki VPA Configuration
- Moved `vpa.yaml` from `cluster/k8s/monitoring/loki/` to new dedicated `cluster/k8s/monitoring/loki-vpa/` directory
- Created new Flux Kustomization for `loki-vpa` with proper dependencies on `loki` and `vpa`
- Updated main cluster kustomization to reference the new `loki-vpa` Kustomization

### CRD Validation
- Updated `crd_layering.py` to reference `flux-image-automation-ghcr` instead of generic `flux-image-automation`
- Added TODO comment for future non-GHCR image automation support

### Pre-commit Configuration
- Added `urllib3` as an additional dependency for Checkov Terraform checks

## Implementation Details
- Error recovery messages are dynamically populated with the session ID for better user guidance
- Daemon HTTP errors now include both status code and response body for debugging
- VPA configuration is now properly isolated in its own Kustomization with explicit dependencies

## 横向对比

你好！我是专注于 AI 开发工具生态的技术分析师。基于 2026-04-07 的社区动态数据，我为你整理了这份横向对比分析报告。

---

# 2026-04-07 主流 AI CLI 工具横向对比分析报告

## 1. 生态全景
当前 AI CLI 工具已从单一的“聊天外壳”演变为**全生命周期开发伙伴**。今日的动态显示，行业竞争焦点已从单纯的模型能力转向**工程化体验的深水区**。主要呈现三大态势：一是**多智能体编排**成为核心战场，如何在终端优雅地展示并发任务和解决状态同步是各家的痛点；二是**成本与效率的博弈**，用户对 Token 消耗异常（如 Codex、Kimi）和上下文管理（Claude Code 历史丢失）极度敏感；三是**协议与标准化**，MCP (Model Context Protocol) 正迅速成为事实标准，除 Gemini CLI 原生支持外，OpenCode、Charm Crush 等均在深度集成。

---

## 2. 各工具活跃度对比

| 工具名称 | 今日 Issues (活跃讨论) | 今日 PRs (迭代速度) | 版本发布状态 | 核心稳定性 |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :--- | :--- |
| **Claude Code** | 🔥 **极高** (8个热点, 计费/UX争议) | 低 (无新合并) | 无更新 | ⚠️ 争议大 (v2.1.89 UX回退) |
| **OpenAI Codex** | 🔥 **高** (内存泄漏/Token浪费) | 🚀 **极高** (10+个重构) | v0.119.0-alpha | ⚠️ Alpha阶段, 内存问题 |
| **Charm Crush** | 🔥 **中** (Undo/Redo/本地模型) | 🚀 **高** (功能补齐中) | **v0.55.1** (修复) | ✅ 稳定 (快速响应) |
| **Gemini CLI** | 🔥 **高** (权限/订阅识别) | 🔥 **高** (架构重构) | v0.36.0-nightly | ⚠️ 配置复杂度增加 |
| **GitHub Copilot CLI**| 🔵 **低** (存量清理) | ❌ 无 | **v1.0.19** (维护) | ✅ 成熟稳定 |
| **Kimi Code CLI** | 🔥 **中** (重构讨论/Windows适配) | 🔥 **中** (关键修复) | 无更新 | ⚠️ 兼容性挑战 |
| **OpenCode** | 🔥 **高** (模型适配/快捷键) | 🔥 **高** (MCP/Rich UI) | **v1.3.17** (热修) | ✅ 迭代迅速 |
| **Qwen Code** | 🔥 **高** (多Agent闪屏/审查) | 🔥 **高** (体验增强) | ❌ 构建失败 | ⚠️ 并发交互Bug |

---

items.title: Strategic Directive: Evolution to Universal Agent Infrastructure (2026-07-25)
items.content:
This submission implements the full Daily Automation workflow for the MCP Any architectural evolution. 

1. **Market Sync**: Identified OpenClaw's Cognitive Honeypots, Claude Code's mandatory RPW, and Gemini CLI's SII as key ecosystem shifts.
2. **Strategic Vision**: Pivoted toward Proactive Cognitive Interdiction and Fragment-Level Traceability to counter "Hallucination Hijacking" and "Semantic Erasure."
3. **Feature Inventory**: Added P0 priorities for CHP, RPW Hub, and SII Isolator.
4. **Design Docs**: Created a Google-Standard design doc for the Cognitive Honeypot Provider (CHP) and evolved the Reasoning Provenance Validator to include RPW hooks.
5. **Roadmaps**: Synchronized engineering pipelines for both Server and UI to reflect these shifts.

All changes adhere to the non-destructive, append-only, and strict formatting rules required for Senior Architect caliber delivery.

items.author: VannaDii

items.title: fix(slack): forward resolved token to downloadSlackFile
items.content:
When `botToken` is configured as a SecretRef (e.g. `{ "$ref": "slack-bot-token" }`), the `downloadFile` action fails with "File could not be downloaded (not found, too large, or inaccessible)" even though the file exists and the bot has access.

The problem is in the call chain:

1. `handleSlackAction` resolves the account from the gateway's runtime config snapshot, where SecretRefs are already resolved to plain strings
2. `buildActionOpts("read")` builds options for read operations — but when the resolved token equals `botToken`, it omits the `token` field (to avoid redundant overrides)
3. `downloadSlackFile` receives opts without a token, calls `resolveToken()` internally, which calls `loadConfig()` — returning the **raw** config with unresolved SecretRef dicts
4. `resolveSlackBotToken` gets the dict instead of a string, fails, and the error is caught and mapped to a null return

Other actions (sendMessage, readMessages, etc.) work because they don't re-resolve the token through `loadConfig()` in the same way.

**Fix:** explicitly pass the already-resolved token from `getTokenForOperation("read")` when calling `downloadSlackFile`, so its internal `resolveToken` receives a string and skips the raw config path.

**Tests added:**
- bot token is forwarded when no userToken override exists
- userToken is forwarded when configured
- no token is injected when neither is available

Closes #62088

items.title: Add OpenClaw project breakdown and comparison with Claude Code and opencode
items.content:
Adds `OPENCLAW_BREAKDOWN.md` — a detailed reference covering OpenClaw's architecture, capabilities, and how it positions against Claude Code (Anthropic) and opencode (community Go CLI).

### Document structure

- **What OpenClaw is** — self-hosted, MIT-licensed AI agent platform (Node.js/TypeScript/Electron); any LLM backend; free BYOK
- **What it does** — coding agent, system automation, multi-channel messaging (WhatsApp/Slack/Telegram/Discord), 100+ skill plugins
- **How it works** — hub-and-spoke event-driven architecture: Message Router → Gateway Daemon → LLM abstraction + Docker-sandboxed execution layer; TypeScript plugin SDK with hot-reload; JSONL action transcripts
- **Claude Code** — Anthropic proprietary CLI; best-in-class subagent orchestration and deep code reasoning; Anthropic-only, no self-hosting, paid tiers
- **opencode** — MIT Go CLI; 75+ LLM providers including local Ollama; client/server persistence; 120K+ stars; coding-only scope
- **Comparison tables** — feature matrix, architecture diff, use-case routing, and philosophy comparison across all three tools
items.author: Copilot

items.title: fix: isolate cron jobs on Haiku, separate query from delivery
items.content:
Resolves #289

## The Design Tension (why PR #290 failed)

PR #290 got stuck in a 4+ iteration review loop because of a fundamental contradiction the reviewers correctly identified each time:

- **Security/Prompt review** blocked moving `reminder-check` to `sessionTarget: isolated` because reminder delivery requires the main session surface — an isolated session can't reach the user.
- **Design review** blocked keeping `reminder-check` on `sessionTarget: main` because the PR title/issue promised both jobs would move to isolated.

The PR flip-flopped between these positions without ever resolving the underlying tension. The merge decision agent kept applying contradictory fixes from alternating reviewers.

## The Deliberate Choice: Separate Query from Delivery

This PR resolves the tension by cleanly separating the cheap query work from user-facing delivery:

**Query (isolated Haiku, cheap):**
- Both `reminder-check` and `pull-main` now run as `sessionTarget: isolated` with `model: litellm/claude-haiku-4-5`
- `reminder-check` only runs `check-reminders.sh` and writes the handoff file — it never delivers
- `pull-main` only runs `pull-main.sh` — it no longer attempts inline cron spec reapplication

**Delivery (existing mechanisms, no new cron):**
1. **AGENTS.md step 5** (opportunistic): every time the user interacts, the main session checks for the handoff file and delivers immediately
2. **HEARTBEAT.md Check 1** (guaranteed): the heartbeat reads the handoff file every 60 minutes and delivers any stranded reminders

No isolated session ever attempts to deliver reminders to the user.

## Trade-off Accepted

Worst-case reminder delivery latency increases from ~15 minutes to ~75 minutes when the user is fully idle (up to 15 min for cron to write the handoff, then up to 60 min for heartbeat to deliver). In practice:
- Most reminders deliver on the next user interaction (seconds to minutes)
- The current system already can't interrupt mid-conversation (cron only fires when

items.title: [Bug]: openclaw agent CLI returns intermittent empty payloads for ACP agents (pi, opencode) — session/new endpoint issue
items.content:
## Bug type
Behavior bug (intermittent failure)

## Summary
The `openclaw agent --agent <name>` CLI command intermittently returns empty payloads (`payloads: []`) for ACP agents (pi, opencode) even when the agent successfully processes the request. The Gateway logs show \"No reply from agent.\" despite the agent actually running and producing output.

This appears to be related to the session/new endpoint in the OpenClaw ACP backend.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Run: `openclaw agent --agent pi --message \"Reply with exactly TEST_OK\" --json --timeout 60`
2. Run: `openclaw agent --agent opencode --message \"Reply with exactly TEST_OK\" --json --timeout 60` (immediately after)
3. Observe: Sometimes opencode returns empty payloads despite exit code 0

## Environment
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.5 (3e72c03)
- ACP enabled: true
- ACP backend: acpx
- ACP allowedAgents: [\"pi\", \"opencode\"]
- Both pi and opencode configured with model: ollama/qwen3.5:cloud

## Root cause analysis
- The health check script (`acp_runtime_health_check.py`) explicitly documents: \"Uses opencode agent directly (openclaw ACP backend has a session/new bug)\"
- Both pi and opencode use the SAME model — not model-specific
- The issue is timing/concurrency related — running two `openclaw agent` commands in quick succession triggers the bug
- Manual retests with delay succeed consistently
- Using `acpx <agent> exec` directly bypasses the bug

## Evidence
Gateway log during failure:
```
Apr 07 01:45:17 moltbot node[2445724]: 2026-04-07T01:45:17.763+10:00 PI_SMOKE_CRON_OK
Apr 07 01:45:35 moltbot node[2445724]: 2026-04-07T01:45:35.611+10:00 No reply from agent.
```

Only pi output was captured in logs — opencode output was silently dropped.

## Expected behavior
`openclaw agent --agent <acp-agent>` should consistently return the agent's response payloads.

## Workaround
Use `acpx <agent> exec \"<message>\"` directly instead of `openclaw agent`:
```bash
# Instead of:
openclaw agent --agent opencode --message \"test

items.author: tadanobutubutu

items.title: [Bug]: `openclaw sessions --json` hangs when stdout is not a TTY on 2026.4.5
items.content:
## Summary
`openclaw sessions --json` hangs indefinitely when stdout is not a TTY on OpenClaw 2026.4.5.

This reproduces consistently on macOS (arm64) with Volta-managed Node. The same machine does **not** reproduce the issue on 2026.3.28.

## Environment
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.5 (`3e72c03`)
- Previous working version on same machine: 2026.3.28 (`f9b1079`)
- OS: macOS / Darwin 25.2.0 (arm64)
- Node: v22.22.1
- Install method: Volta-managed CLI

## Impact
This breaks non-interactive / automation use of the sessions CLI, including:
- shell redirection
- pipes
- subprocess capture in Python / other runtimes
- cron / scripts / audit tooling

`health --json` still works under the same non-TTY conditions, so this appears specific to the `sessions --json` code path rather than a generic JSON-output failure.

## Reproduction
The following all hang on 2026.4.5:

```bash
openclaw sessions --json > /tmp/sessions.json
openclaw sessions --json | head -n 5
python3 - <<'PY2'
import subprocess
subprocess.run(
    ["openclaw", "sessions", "--json"],
    stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
    stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
    timeout=20,
)
PY2
```

Control case that succeeds on the same machine:

```bash
openclaw health --json > /tmp/health.json
```

## Observed behavior
- `sessions --json > file` hangs and leaves a 0-byte output file
- `sessions --json | head` hangs with no output
- `subprocess.run(..., stdout=PIPE)` hangs until timeout
- no useful stderr is emitted

## Expected behavior
`openclaw sessions --json` should emit JSON and exit normally regardless of whether stdout is a TTY, pipe, redirect target, or captured stream.

## Additional note
This looks like a regression in 2026.4.5 rather than a local config/data corruption issue, because:
1. 2026.3.28 works on the same machine
2. 2026.4.5 reproduces the hang in isolated execution
3. other commands such as `health --json`, `config validate`, `doctor`, and `gateway status --json` still work

items.author: steipete

items.title: Add Burgess Principle Layer – lightweight human-review upgrade for skills
items.content:
### What this adds
A new optional "Burgess Principle Layer" that upgrades skills in this collection with a simple, zero-energy human-review gate.

**Core question:**
“Was a human member of the team able to personally review the specific facts of my specific case?”

### Why it is added
Many skills work great for routine tasks, but on edge cases (accessibility needs, reasonable adjustments, high-stakes decisions, disability-related situations) it is important that the agent does not default to purely automated or templated responses.

The Burgess Principle adds a calm, respectful, lightweight check that encourages the agent to “see the human first” exactly where it matters most, without adding bloat or slowing down normal operation.

### How it works
- The layer is fully optional.
- On genuine edge cases, the skill applies the Burgess binary and suggests respectful follow-up language or human review prompts.
- All original skills remain untouched and fully functional.
- Tone remains calm, human-first, and consistent with the Advocate Companion.

### Example usage
Users can trigger it naturally with prompts like:
- “Review this contract using Burgess Principle”
- “Help with a reasonable adjustment request”
- “Apply Burgess to this DSAR”

Related main repository (full framework and philosophy):  
https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle

Happy to refine or adjust based on feedback.

This is my first PR to this project — thank you for the opportunity and for reviewing the contributing guidelines.

items.author: ljbudgie

items.title: [Idea] OpenClaw 多 Session 编排层：基于 SQLite 的任务状态管理
items.content:
## 背景

ECC 2.0 Alpha 发布了一个 Rust 实现的多 Session 控制平面，核心是 SQLite 持久化 + Terminal UI dashboard + daemon 模式。这套架构解决了 AI Coding 场景中多任务并行、状态恢复、风险可见性的问题。

当前 OpenClaw 的 Session 管理相对线性，缺少统一的 Session Store 来追踪多个并行的 agent 任务及其风险状态。

## 目标

为 OpenClaw 设计一个类似 ECC 2.0 的多 Session 编排层：

- **SQLite Session Store**：持久化每个 agent task 的状态（running/stopped/failed/success）
- **Dashboard UI**：Terminal TUI，显示所有 active sessions、risk score、token 消耗
- **Daemon Mode**：后台持续运行，支持 start/stop/resume/retry 任意 session
- **Worktree-aware**：每个 session 绑定独立的工作目录，避免互相干扰

## 子任务

- [ ] 设计 SQLite schema（session_id, task_desc, status, risk_score, start_time, end_time, worktree_path, token_used）
- [ ] 实现 SessionStore 模块（Rust 或 Go，或 Node.js）
- [ ] 开发 Terminal Dashboard（TUI，支持多 session 实时视图）
- [ ] 实现 daemon loop，支持后台运行和事件回调
- [ ] 集成 OpenClaw 的 agent spawning 机制，自动创建 session records
- [ ] 实现 resume/stop/retry 命令

## 价值

- 多任务并行时不再迷失状态
- 失败任务可精确 retry，而不是重新开始整个对话
- 风险可见 → 高风险任务自动告警或暂停

## 参考

- ECC 2.0: https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/tree/main/ecc2

items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: fix: resolve weekly tracker data loss and display bugs
items.content:
## Summary

- **Week grid wrong-day alignment**: `getWeekEntries()` returned a compact array (only populated entries), but the component indexed by day-of-week position. Entries showed under wrong day columns. Fixed by returning a 7-element array with `null` for missing days.
- **Timezone mismatch**: Server used UTC (`toISOString`) while users expected local dates. A US Pacific user logging at 8 PM Monday would save as Tuesday UTC. Fixed by sending local date from frontend and making server-side date functions consistent with date-fns `format()`.
- **Silent data loss**: `saveJSON`/`saveText` swallowed all DB errors — a failed write returned success, then the refetch showed old data. Fixed by removing try/catch so errors propagate to API callers.
- **Stale form state**: `useState` initial values from `todaysEntry` didn't update when props changed after logging. Fixed with `useEffect` sync (guarded against overwriting mid-edit).
- **Duplicate weekly reviews**: No upsert logic — multiple reviews for the same week were stored, with `getWeekSummary` only reading the first (stale) one for revenue. Fixed with `findIndex` + replace-or-push.
- **No date validation**: API accepted arbitrary strings as date parameter. Added regex + calendar validation returning 400 for invalid dates.

### User Flows

**Happy path:** User opens dashboard → clicks "Log Today" → enters deep work hours, pipeline actions, training status → submits → entry appears under correct day column → data persists on reload

**Failure paths:**
- DB write fails → error propagates to API → frontend receives 500 → user sees error (previously: silent success then data vanishes)
- Invalid date sent → API returns 400 with clear error message
- User submits review twice → upserts instead of duplicating
- User is mid-edit when background refresh happens → form state preserved (not overwritten)
- Week boundary (Sunday→Monday) → previous week data still accessible via Weekly tab comparison

## Evidence

```
$ npm test
items.author: nadvolod

items.title: False positive: swarm-sprint flagged as suspicious — git worktree orchestration skill
items.content:
## Skill

https://clawhub.ai/jdh3/swarm-sprint

## What it does

Swarm Sprint is a parallel coding sprint coordinator for OpenClaw agents. It:

1. Runs a conflict analysis on a task list before any agents are spawned
2. Creates isolated git worktrees (one per task) using `git worktree add`
3. Generates agent task packages written to `swarm-packages.json`
4. After agents complete, removes all worktrees via `git worktree remove` and deletes branches

The script (`scripts/swarm.js`) uses Node.js `child_process.spawnSync` to run standard git commands locally. No network calls, no credentials, no external APIs.

## Why it's likely flagged

The script uses:
- `child_process.spawnSync` — to run `git worktree` commands
- `fs.writeFileSync` / `fs.readFileSync` — to write `swarm-packages.json` and a sprint log
- `fs.mkdirSync` — to create the memory log directory

All of these are standard Node.js dev tooling patterns. There is no network access, no credential handling, no data exfiltration of any kind.

## Request

Manual review and clearance of swarm-sprint@1.0.1. Happy to make changes if the scanner has specific concerns.

items.title: [提效想法] 借鉴 gstack-session-update 实现 AI Coding 后台自动更新机制
items.content:
## 背景

gstack v0.15.7 引入了 ：
- SessionStart hook 触发
- 后台 fork（零延迟）
- 节流：1次/小时
- lockfile 保护（PID + stale recovery）
- GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 静默

当前 AI Coding 工具（Claude Code、OpenClaw）更新依赖手动触发或全局定时，缺乏会话级自动更新能力。

## 目标

实现一个轻量级后台更新机制，在 AI Coding 会话启动时静默检查并更新，保持工具始终是最新版本。

## 子任务

- [ ] 设计会话级更新 Hook（参考 gstack-session-update）
- [ ] 实现 PID lockfile + stale lock recovery
- [ ] 实现 1次/小时节流控制
- [ ] 实现静默更新（GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0）
- [ ] 集成到 OpenClaw agent 启动流程
- [ ] 增量更新 vs 全量更新的策略选择

items.title: feat: keyword-based message routing for pre-turn model selection
items.content:
## Summary

Add zero-cost pre-turn model routing that selects the LLM based on keyword/pattern matching in the incoming message, before any LLM call.

## Motivation

Different tasks benefit from different models (coding → claude-sonnet, research → gemini-flash, quick Q&A → qwen35). Currently requires manual model switching.

## Proposed Design

```typescript
messageRouting: {
  rules: [
    { match: ["code review", "PR"], model: "github-copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6" },
    { match: ["research", "search"], model: "gemini-3-flash" },
  ],
  default: "qwen35",
}
```

- First-match-wins rule ordering
- Case-insensitive substring matching
- Zero LLM cost — pure string matching
- Falls back to primary model if no rule matches

## Implementation

Prototype: scottgl9/nanoclaw feature/message-routing-20260406
- src/message-routing.ts — types + resolveMessageRoutingModel()
- src/message-routing.test.ts — 6 tests passing
- src/types.ts — messageRouting field added
- src/index.ts — wired into agent dispatch

Also implemented in openclaw/openclaw#62153 and scottgl9/LeanClaw#7.
items.author: scottgl9

items.title: refactor(blog-runner): extract path and artifact services (#260)
items.content:
Closes #260. Part of epic #250 (blog-runner modularization).

## Summary

Installs the PR2 seam for Program #250 by extracting path resolution and artifact naming/lookup out of `blog-pipeline/src/blog_runner/_runner.py` into a new `blog_runner/services/` package, without changing any caller semantics. This is a pure seam install, not a thinning pass — `_runner.py` grows by 17 lines (17394 → 17411, +0.098%) because delegate bodies plus the compatibility shim offset the table move.

This PR is the first one that actually moves runtime logic out of the monolith. PR1 (#259/#285) added the parallel-view typed contracts (`PipelineContext`, `RunState`, `PathState`, `ArtifactState`, `ArtifactRef`, `GateDecision`) but no runtime consumer. PR2 gives those contracts a real home.

## Root cause

Two coupled problems the path/artifact surface has been carrying since before PR1:

1. **Monolithic path helpers.** 14 private `_*_path` helpers live at `_runner.py:1399–1475`, plus two promotion helpers, plus a plural-form aggregator. Every downstream PR in the #250 epic — PR3 (recovery/MC/gates), PR5+ (numbered step extraction), PR8 (graphics runtime) — needs to reach back into these private helpers until there is a real service behind them. Leaving them inline defeats the modularization.

2. **Latent `STRUCTURED_ARTIFACT_PATHS` drift bug.** The table was defined twice:
   - `_runner.py:343` — 23 entries, canonical, used by every internal helper.
   - `_types.py:181` — 20 entries, missing `placeholder-descriptions.json`, `link-check-results.json`, and `mc-report-fallback.jsonl`, and pointing `og-result.json` and `image-manifest.json` at the wrong directories.

   `blog_runner/__init__.py` re-exports the `_types.py` version, so any external consumer that imports `STRUCTURED_ARTIFACT_PATHS` from the package namespace silently got the broken table while internal callers got the canonical one. PR2 owns artifact-name resolution, so PR2 is the only correct place to collapse this drift — lea

items.title: Remove nullable unions, genericize runbook paths, and dedupe modal listeners
items.content:
### Motivation
- The JSON Schema used nullable union types (`["string","null"]`) which violates repository schema constraints and can break downstream tooling. 
- The repair runbook included environment-specific absolute and home-relative paths which should be deployment-agnostic for security and reusability. 
- `showModal()` in the mission control UI attached event listeners on every open which caused handler stacking, duplicate invocations, and potential memory leaks. 

### Description
- Replace nullable unions in `eldon/action_allowlist/action_schema.json` by making `last_executed_at`, `last_reviewed_at`, and `review_due_date` plain optional string fields. 
- Replace absolute paths in the canonical deployment table in `deploy/repair/openclaw_repair_runbook.md` with placeholders such as `<OPENCLAW_ROOT>`, `<OPENCLAW_ENV_FILE>`, `<SYSTEMD_UNIT_PATH>`, `<OPENCLAW_DATA_DIR>`, and `<OPENCLAW_BACKUP_DIR>`. 
- Add a module-level `activeModalCloseHandler` in `mission-control/mc.js`, change `showModal()` to set that handler rather than attach listeners, and register the `modal-close` and `modal-backdrop` click listeners once to invoke the active handler. 

### Testing
- Parsed the updated schema with `node -e "const fs=require('fs'); JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('eldon/action_allowlist/action_schema.json','utf8'))"` and it succeeded. 
- Performed a syntax check with `node --check mission-control/mc.js` and no errors were reported. 
- Ran repository lint and formatter via the commit helper which executed `oxlint` and `oxfmt` and completed successfully, with only a Node engine version warning from the environment.

------

items.title: Gateway crashes from unhandled promise: 'Agent listener invoked outside active run'
items.content:
## Bug Report

**Version:** 2026.4.5 (3e72c03)
**Node:** v25.6.1
**OS:** Linux 6.17.13-2-pve (x64), LXC container

### Description

The gateway process crashes with an unhandled promise rejection when an orphaned exec subprocess writes to stdout/stderr after the agent run has ended. This causes the gateway to exit with status 1, and systemd restarts it — which can create a crash loop if the orphan process writes repeatedly.

### Reproduction

1. Start the gateway with any agent that has exec-based tools
2. A cron job or session spawns an exec child process
3. The gateway restarts (SIGUSR1 for config change, or crashes for another reason)
4. The exec child process survives the restart (gets reparented to PID 1)
5. When the orphan process writes to its stdout/stderr pipe, the agent event handler fires `Agent.processEvents` outside of an active run
6. Unhandled promise rejection → Node.js exits with code 1

### Stack Trace

```
[openclaw] Unhandled promise rejection: Error: Agent listener invoked outside active run
    at Agent.processEvents (node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-agent-core/src/agent.ts:533:10)
    at node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-agent-core/src/agent.ts:380:21
    at Object.onUpdate (node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-agent-core/src/agent-loop.ts:539:7)
    at emitUpdate (dist/exec-defaults-uj0McX2k.js:1524:8)
    at handleStdout (dist/exec-defaults-uj0McX2k.js:1546:4)
    at Object.onSupervisorStdout [as onStdout] (dist/exec-defaults-uj0McX2k.js:1610:3)
    ...
    at Socket.<anonymous> (dist/exec-defaults-uj0McX2k.js:568:4)
```

Also observed with `handleStderr` path.

### Observed Impact

- 10 crashes in ~10 minutes when crash loop occurs
- Gateway becomes unresponsive during rapid restart cycles
- All channels (Telegram, Discord) lose connectivity

### Expected Behavior

Either:
1. **Kill child processes on SIGUSR1/restart** — the gateway should terminate all exec subprocess children before restarting in-process
2. **Handle gracefully** — catch the "outside ac
items.author: seanford

items.title: Fix Mission Control gateway connect handshake payload
items.content:
### Motivation
- The gateway requires a v3 connect handshake and explicit operator scopes and client metadata for authorization, but Mission Control's inline client was sending incompatible/connect-missing fields causing the gateway to reject the handshake or subsequent RPCs.
- Aligning the connect payload prevents missing-scope rejections for read/write/approvals/pairing methods and ensures the gateway receives expected metadata.

### Description
- Set `minProtocol` and `maxProtocol` to `3` in the Mission Control `connect` request to match the gateway protocol version.
- Add `role: "operator"` and a `scopes` array including `operator.read`, `operator.write`, `operator.approvals`, and `operator.pairing` to the `connect` params.
- Include standard connect metadata fields `caps: []`, `userAgent: navigator.userAgent`, and `locale: navigator.language` while preserving conditional token auth.
- Change applied to `mission-control/index.html` and formatted via the repo tooling.

### Testing
- Ran `scripts/committer "Fix Mission Control gateway connect framing and scopes" mission-control/index.html`, which invoked the repo linter and formatter (`oxlint` and `oxfmt`), and those steps completed without errors.
- Verified presence of `type: "req"` framing and the added `operator.read` scope via `rg` searches, which returned the updated lines as expected.
- No unit or integration test suites were modified or executed as part of this change; automated lint/format checks passed.

------

items.title: Fix Mission Control config patch safety, deep-merge agent updates, and no-op writes
items.content:
### Motivation
- Remove an unsafe `eval` fallback that parsed `config.get.raw` in the browser, which allowed arbitrary JS execution when JSON.parse failed. 
- Prevent shallow-merge clobbers when updating nested per-agent fields like `tools` and `subagents` which could drop important configuration keys. 
- Make the config editor resilient to JSON5 source text and avoid writing empty/no-op patches that would trigger unnecessary restarts. 

### Description
- Removed `eval`-based parsing and changed `buildAgentPatch` to accept parsed config data instead of reparsing raw text, implemented `isPlainObject` and `deepMergeObjects` to recursively merge nested agent updates. 
- Updated Mission Control components (`ToolPolicyPanel`, `TopologyTab`, `AgentDetail`, `ConfigSchemaForm`, and `App`) to use a new `configData` (parsed config object) state propagated from `config.get` instead of reparsing `configRaw`. 
- Hardened `ConfigSchemaForm` to initialize fields from parsed config, accept JSON5 raw patch text (without `JSON.parse`-only validation), reject empty raw edits client-side, and skip sending empty `{}` form patches. 
- Added gateway-side no-op detection in `config.patch` using `isDeepStrictEqual` to avoid writing the config file, creating restart sentinel, or scheduling a restart when the effective config is unchanged. 
- Modified files: `mission-control/index.html` (client UI logic, `buildAgentPatch`, `configData` wiring, editor checks) and `src/gateway/server-methods/config.ts` (no-op check in `config.patch`). 

### Testing
- Ran `pnpm vitest src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts`, which completed successfully (8 tests passed). 
- Ran formatting and lint checks (`pnpm prettier --check` and repo formatter via the commit flow); initial style warnings were fixed with `prettier --write` / repo format tools and checks passed. 
- Attempted to run the `config.patch` e2e test (`src/gateway/server.config-patch.e2e.test.ts`) but `*.e2e.test.ts` files are excluded by the repo Vitest c

items.title: Validate origin and origin/main in pr-env-doctor; avoid printing remote URLs
items.content:
### Motivation
- The doctor previously passed when any git remote existed, but PR tooling requires a remote named `origin` and a reachable `origin/main` branch. 
- Printing `git remote -v` can expose embedded credentials in remote URLs. 
- Make environment checks stricter and remediation guidance explicit to avoid later failures in PR scripts. 

### Description
- Replace the permissive remote presence check with `git remote get-url origin` and print only remote names via `git remote` to avoid URL leakage. 
- Add `git ls-remote --exit-code origin refs/heads/main` to verify `origin/main` is reachable. 
- Update failure messages and suggested fixes to explicitly instruct adding an `origin` remote and fetching `origin/main`. 

### Testing
- Ran `bash -n scripts/pr-env-doctor` to validate the script syntax and it passed. 
- Executed `scripts/pr-env-doctor` in the current environment and observed expected failure outputs for missing `origin` and missing `gh`, validating the new checks and messaging.

items.title: Fail ops-hardening check when `.env` is tracked
items.content:
### Motivation
- Prevent accidental inclusion of sensitive credentials in the repo by failing the ops hardening check when a tracked `.env` file is present. 

### Description
- Added a git-backed guard in `scripts/ops-hardening-check.ts` that invokes `git ls-files --error-unmatch .env` (via `execFile`/`promisify`) and appends a clear failure message if `.env` is tracked, while leaving the existing pipeline-operator shell checks unchanged. 

### Testing
- Ran `node --import tsx scripts/ops-hardening-check.ts` and observed the script complete with `ops-hardening check passed`, and automated lint/format hooks ran as part of the local validation.

items.title: Fix messaging allowlist parsing and dedup cache pruning
items.content:
### Motivation
- Fix allowlist parsing where `MESSAGING_ALLOWED_RECIPIENTS` / `IMESSAGE_ALLOWED_RECIPIENTS` were split without trimming, producing empty or whitespace entries and causing incorrect allowlist behavior. 
- Prevent unbounded memory growth in `MessagePolicy._recent_hashes` which could accumulate stale dedup entries in long-running processes and lead to OOM.

### Description
- Trim and filter recipient entries in `MessagingConfig.from_env()` so empty or whitespace-only values are not stored, by using `recipient.strip()` and filtering falsy results. 
- Replace the unbounded `defaultdict(float)` with a typed `dict[str, float]` for `_recent_hashes` and add a `_prune_recent_hashes(now: float)` helper to evict hashes older than the configured `dedup_window_minutes`. 
- Invoke `_prune_recent_hashes()` from both `allow()` and `record_send()` to keep the dedup cache bounded during lookups and writes. 
- Add unit tests in `eldon/tests/test_messaging.py` to cover env allowlist parsing, empty allowlist behavior, and dedup pruning.

### Testing
- Ran the focused test suite with `PYTHONPATH=src:. pytest -q tests/test_messaging.py`, resulting in `19 passed, 1 warning` (success). 
- A plain `pytest -q tests/test_messaging.py` run failed at collection due to import path in the local environment (`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'openclaw'`), which is an environment invocation issue and not related to the code changes. 
- New tests added and executed: `test_config_from_env_strips_and_filters_allowlist`, `test_config_from_env_empty_allowlist`, and `test_policy_prunes_old_dedup_entries`, all passing under the tested command.

items.title: telegram: send single complete reply (no chunking)
items.content:
### Motivation
- External messaging channels must not receive partial or streaming replies, so Telegram outbound messages should be delivered as a single complete reply.
- Existing behavior split long replies into sequential messages, which violates the channel semantics and compliance requirements.
- Keep retry/backoff behavior for transient errors while preventing chunked/partial updates.

### Description
- Updated module docs to state that `send()` sends complete replies only (no chunking).
- Replaced chunked-send logic in `TelegramConnector.send()` with single-message semantics and a length check against `_MAX_MSG_LEN`.
- When a reply exceeds Telegram's `4096` character limit the connector logs a warning and sends a single fallback message: `"Response too long to send to Telegram in a single message."`.
- Retained existing retry/backoff behavior by continuing to use the `_send_chunk()` helper for the single outbound message.

### Testing
- Ran `python -m compileall eldon/src/openclaw/connectors/telegram.py`, which succeeded.
- Ran repository lint/type-aware checks via the committer flow (`oxlint`), which reported `0` warnings and `0` errors for the modified file.
